# CBS Group Tender: TfNSW AMSS RFP (Asset Management Support Services)

> Transport for NSW Asset Management Support Services Request for Proposal - CBS Group's conforming offer and three alternative offers including value-based pricing models.

**Source folder:** `/home/ubuntu/Downloads/dropbox_raw/CBS_RAG_extracted/Proposals/03. Final Submission`

**Export date:** April 2026

## CBS Group - Response to Condition of Participation - RFP No. WS5364262133.docx

*File: `CBS Group - Response to Condition of Participation - RFP No. WS5364262133.docx`*

8th December 2025

David Hanna
Strategic Sourcing Manager
Transport for NSW
Level 2, 7 Harvest Street
Macquarie Park NSW 2113

Dear David,

Re: Response to Condition of Participation - RFP No. WS5364262133

Thank you for your email regarding the Condition of Participation for the Asset Management Support Services RFP (WS5364262133). We confirm that our proposal, submitted on 1st December 2025, addresses this condition in full.

As a summary of our compliance, our submission details extensive experience across the required areas of expertise. This includes developing video-only tolling models and whole-of-life costings for the WestConnex, Sydney Harbour Tunnel, and M6 projects; leading technical and commercial due diligence for these same assets; and creating long-term, 30-year asset management models and lifecycle costings. Furthermore, our experience extends to asset commissioning and transition on major projects such as the WestConnex Motorways (M4, M5, and M8), ensuring comprehensive readiness from construction to operation and managing end-of-contract transitions.

To further assist the project team with their evaluation, we have attached a detailed appendix. This document maps each specific Condition of Participation to the precise locations within our proposal where our compliance and experience are detailed, providing:

The location of the formal compliance statement in our proposal.

The specific sections where each condition is addressed in detail.

A summary of how each condition has been met, including relevant project experience.

We trust this provides the necessary clarity for your evaluation. Please do not hesitate to contact me if you require any further information.

Regards,

Sarah Taylor
Partner, Strategy & Technology
CBS Group Pty Lrd
sarah@cbs.com.au
+61 433 704 246

Attachment:

CBS Detailed Conditions Precedent Matrix


| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| Item | Condition of participation | Compliance Table | Specific Response Location(s) | Summary of how condition has been met |

| 1 | Asset management input to systems and tolling, evaluation and WOL costing for road or rail tunnels | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: We have demonstrated experience across three named transactions. 
Transaction 1 (WHT): Developed the video-only tolling strategy, TaaS model and Whole-of-Life (WOL) costings through the definition, procurement and current delivery.
Transaction 2 (HCTP): Developed the video-only tolling strategy, technical specification as a precursor model and Whole-of-Life (WOL) costings through the definition, procurement and current delivery.
Transaction 3 (M6): Provided ongoing systems and tolling advice. |

| 2 | Asset Due Diligence | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: We have led asset due diligence on three named transactions. 
Transaction 1 (SHT): Led technical and commercial due diligence of the SHT in preparation for the Asset Manager engagement. 
Transaction 2 (WHT): Led technical and commercial due diligence of the WHT through design and delivery
Transaction 3 (CRN): Led technical and commercial due diligence for the potential tender for asset management of the CRN. |

| 3 | Pre-Tender Estimates for 30 plus year Asset Management or PPP contracts in road or rail | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: We have developed pre-tender estimates for 30+ year contracts on three named transactions. 
Transaction 1 (WHT): Developed pre-tender estimates and the 30-year fixed-fee asset management model, tolling solution and re-calculated for revised AM scope of services. 
Transaction 2 (SHT): Developed pre-tender estimates and the 30-year fixed-fee asset management model and re-calculated for revised scope.
Transaction 3 (M6): Developed pre-tender estimates and the 30-year fixed-fee asset management model. |

| 4 | Life Cycle Cost Modelling for tunnels | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: We have developed detailed lifecycle cost models for tunnels on three named transactions – that has become the basis for the TfNSW fixed asset lifecycle cost models. 
Transaction 1 (WHT): Developed detailed lifecycle cost models, including asset specific versions to support parametrised change and design development activities.
Transaction 2 (M6): Developed detailed lifecycle cost models to support innovations proposed for M6.
Transaction 3 (GWH): Developed detailed lifecycle cost models to support the co-lab framework and $1b savings for this proposed project |

| 5 | Asset Management contract procurement processes | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.1: How CBS will deliver the services Section 
5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: We provided extensive support to the design of and procurement for three named transactions. 
Transaction 1 (WHT): Specification, terms sheet, evaluation participation through from market engagement to contract award. 
Transaction 2 (SHT): Specification, terms sheet, evaluation participation through from market engagement to contract award.
Transaction 3 (M6): Specification, terms sheet, evaluation participation through from market engagement to contract award. |

| 6 | Asset Commissioning | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: We have extensive experience in asset commissioning for major tunnel projects across at least three named transactions. 
Transaction 1 (WestConnex M4): Developed operational readiness plans. 
Transaction 2 (WestConnex M5): Managed the transition from construction to operation. 
Transaction 3 (WestConnex M8): Managed the transition from construction to operation. |

| 7 | Parametrised change processes | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.1: How CBS will deliver the services. 
Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: Our team designed the parameterised change process that is incorporate into the AM contracts.  We have applied this methodology as follows:
Transaction 1 (WHT): Application and refinement of the LCCM and commercial provisions to ascertain VfM as part of the two contract baselines using the paramterised change method.  
Transaction 2 (M6): Application of the parameterised change method to establish the M6 baseline and support innovations valuation. |

| 8 | Asset Class Strategies | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.1: How CBS will deliver the services | Verified Experience: We have provided strategic advice on asset class strategies across multiple transactions. 
Transaction 1 (Bus Asset Class): Developed the template asset class strategy and strategy architectures that provide the foundation for all asset class strategies at TfNSW
Transaction 2 (Control Systems Asset Class Strategy): Draft development of the road control systems asset class strategy for TfNSW
Transaction 3 (Harbour Crossings and Norther Tunnels): Develop the precinct class strategies for the Harbour Crossings and the Northern Tunnels. |

| 9 | End of current Asset Management/PPP contract/s | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: Our transition-out planning is based on our experience with long-term contracts. 
Transaction 1 (SHT): Our team played a substantial role in the end of SHTC Concession for the SHT and transition to the new AM
Transaction 2: Vic OSARS brownfield PPP support to assess asset condition, risk and establish portfolio risk profile. |

| 10 | Investor Assurance (Gateway) processes | Section 2: Confirmation of Conditions of Participation | Section 5.2: Case Studies | Verified Experience: Our experience with gateway reviews and investor assurance is demonstrated across our major engagements. 
Transaction 1 (WHT): Our team have and continue to support INSW assurance briefings, raising risks, and explaining management strategies
Transaction 2 (M6): Our team have and continue to support INSW assurance briefings, raising risks, and explaining management strategies |

RE: RESPONSE TO CONDITION OF PARTICIPATION - RFP NO. WS5364262133

Thank you for your email regarding the Condition of Participation for the Asset Management Support Services RFP
(WS5364262133). We confirm that our proposal, submitted on 1st December 2025, addresses this condition in
full.

As a summary of our compliance, our submission details extensive experience across the required areas of
expertise. This includes developing video-only tolling models and whole-of-life costings for the WestConnex,
Sydney Harbour Tunnel, and M6 projects; leading technical and commercial due diligence for these same assets;
and creating long-term, 30-year asset management models and lifecycle costings. Furthermore, our experience
extends to asset commissioning and transition on major projects such as the WestConnex Motorways (M4, M5,
and M8), ensuring comprehensive readiness from construction to operation and managing end-of-contract
transitions.

any further information.

Sarah Taylor

## TfNSW AMSS RFP_CBS Group_Alternative Offer 1.pdf

*File: `Final PDFs/TfNSW AMSS RFP_CBS Group_Alternative Offer 1.pdf`*

TRANSPORT FOR NSW (TFNSW)

Alternative Proposal 1
CBS Value Based Pricing
Collaborative Terms and Value Based
Pricing Framework

RFP No. WS5364262133
Date: 1st December 2025
1 December 2025
David Hanna
Strategic Sourcing Manager
Transport for NSW
Level 2, 7 Harvest Street
Macquarie Park NSW 2113


Subject: Alternate Offer 1 for Asset Management Services
Dear David,
On behalf of CBS Group, I am pleased to submit our alternate offer for the provision of Asset Management
services to Transport for NSW. This proposal responds to our discussions regarding innovative commercial
models and may be accepted either as an alternate to our fully compliant offer or as a supplemental
engagement mechanism to meet TfNSW's asset management and value creation needs over the term.
The offer comprises two key elements:
Value Based Pricing
This framework offers a shared-risk model that directly aligns our compensation with the tangible value we
deliver. It moves beyond the conventional client-consultant relationship—where value risk sits entirely with
the client—to a collaborative partnership focused on measurable outcomes.
Collaborative Contract Terms
Our fully compliant offer provides for 100% acceptance of the proposed contract terms. However, these
terms pass risks to CBS Group that come at a cost. We have assessed the value of terms representing
additional risk and propose reduced fees where the amendments in this alternate offer are accepted.
We are confident this framework offers a compelling value proposition: it combines CBS Group's deep local
expertise with global validation capabilities, enabling us to accept risk on value-based pricing while providing
additional service value in exchange for collaborative commercial risk allocation.
We have enclosed a detailed proposal outlining the framework, its operation, and the value it is designed to
create.
Thank you for the opportunity to present this alternate offer. We look forward to discussing it with you further.
Sincerely




Sarah Taylor
Partner, Strategy and Tech
CBS Group Pty Ltd
TABLE OF CONTENTS


Executive summary ............................................................................................................................ 1

1.       Dual engagement pathway framework ..................................................................................... 2

2.       The Case for a New Engagement Model (Method 2) .................................................................... 3

3.       The Value-Based Engagement Model ........................................................................................ 5

4.       Initial (Seed) Initiatives for Consideration ............................................................................... 10

5.       Traditional Engagement Under Collaborative Terms................................................................ 11

6.       Next Steps ............................................................................................................................ 12

Appendix A: Project Schedule Template ............................................................................................ 14

Appendix B: Sample VBP Financial Flow ............................................................................................ 16

Appendix C: Proposed Contract Amendments ................................................................................... 20

Appendix D: Alternate 1 Schedule of Rates ........................................................................................ 25

Appendix E: Catalyst7TM Measurement Framework ............................................................................ 30

Appendix F: VBP Standards Rationalisation Approach........................................................................ 33
Executive summary
Situation: Traditional models of consultant engagement leave all value risk with the client. Consultants are
paid for their time and deliverables regardless of whether their recommendations generate tangible benefits.
This creates a fundamental misalignment of incentives—the consultant's commercial success is
disconnected from the client's actual outcomes. Similarly, standard contract terms allocate risks to the
consultant that come at a cost yet provide no corresponding benefit to the client when those risks do not
materialise.
Complication: Value in complex infrastructure engagements is inherently uncertain. Even well-conceived
initiatives may not deliver anticipated benefits due to implementation challenges, changing circumstances or
unforeseen constraints. Under traditional arrangements, TfNSW bears this uncertainty entirely while
consultants face no consequence for recommendations that fail to materialise as value. Meanwhile, contract
terms that transfer risk to consultants simply result in higher fees to cover contingencies that may never
eventuate—cost without benefit.
Response: CBS Group proposes an alternate engagement model built on two complementary mechanisms.
1. Traditional Engagement on Collaborative Contract Terms: This mechanism provides for engagement of
   our core and supplementary teams on amended commercial terms. TfNSW acceptance of these terms
   allows a reduction in CBS Group costs and risk premium - enabling reduced fees. This creates a direct
   exchange: TfNSW accepts manageable risks in return for tangible cost savings
2. Value Based Price (VBP) Method: Value Based Pricing structures our compensation around shared risk
   and aligned returns, operating on three foundational principles: shared conviction (both parties agree
   there is genuine possibility of value before work commences), co-investment (CBS Group assigns
   appropriate specialists on a fixed retainer basis, with value share agreed initiative-by-initiative), and
   aligned returns (if value is delivered, we share in it; if not, our returns are limited accordingly).
We bring a suite of proven asset management products, methodologies and analytical tools that have
delivered more than $1 billion in validated savings for TfNSW over the past three years. It is with this
capability and track record that we can accept shared risk with confidence.
Mechanism 1 is detailed at section 5 of this Alternate Proposal. The remainder of this document has been
drafted to explain the value and operation of Mechanism 2 (VBP).
We look forward to working through the our seed list of initiatives and the necessary commercial
amendments to rapidly enable this model and start driving real value to support the Asset Financial
Sustainability (AFS) targets set for the organisation.




                                                                                     Page 1
1.          Dual engagement pathway framework
1.1.        Framework structure
This alternative framework is built on two distinct and complementary engagement pathways, allowing
TfNSW to select the most suitable approach for any given initiative.
Method 1: Traditional, Time-Based Engagement
This pathway follows a conventional consultancy model. Services are provided at market rates for a defined
scope of work. This option is suitable for projects where the scope and deliverables are clearly defined from
the outset and where a traditional risk allocation is preferred. Services are provided at market rates for a
defined scope of work. Work orders do not include value share provisions.
Method 2: Shared-Risk, Value-Based Engagement
This pathway is founded on a partnership model. CBSE accepts a significantly reduced monthly retainer,
representing a substantial investment from our side. In return, we participate in the value we help create
through a pre-agreed sharing mechanism on validated benefits. This model ensures that both organisations
share in the risks and rewards, creating a strong alignment of interests.

1.2.        Selection Criteria
The choice between Option A and Option B would be guided by the nature of the project.
•    Method 1 is the preferred choice for more straightforward engagements. This includes defined-scope
     studies, technical investigations, or when seeking short-term expert advice. It is also appropriate for
     engagements where a more conservative, traditional risk allocation is desired
•    Method 2 is particularly well-suited for initiatives with the potential for significant value creation, such as
     long-term transformation programs or projects that require innovative solutions. It is most effective in
     situations where a deep, collaborative partnership and fully aligned incentives are critical to success.

1.3.        Flexibility and Combination
The framework is designed for flexibility, allowing TfNSW to:
•    Employ different options for different projects concurrently
•    Transition between the two options with an agreed-upon notice period
•    Combine both options within a single large-scale program
•    Initiate with a pilot of Option A before committing to a broader rollout




                                                                                         Page 2
2.            The Case for a New Engagement Model (Method 2)
2.1.          The Problem with Traditional Consulting
The conventional consulting model contains a structural flaw that limits its effectiveness for infrastructure
agencies. Under traditional fee-for-service arrangements, consultants are compensated for effort expended
rather than value delivered.
This misalignment creates predictable consequences:

 Characteristic       Traditional Model                              Consequence for TfNSW
 Payment basis        Time and materials or fixed fee for            Payment made regardless of whether recommendations
                      deliverables                                   generate value
 Risk allocation      Client bears all implementation and            TfNSW absorbs losses if initiatives fail to deliver
                      realisation risk                               anticipated benefits
 Consultant           Maximise billable hours or scope               Focus shifts to engagement extension rather than rapid
 incentive                                                           value delivery
 Success              Deliverable completion                         Reports and recommendations may sit unimplemented
 measurement
 Knowledge transfer   Limited incentive to build client capability   Dependency on consultant for ongoing support

Infrastructure asset management initiatives involve inherent uncertainty. Even expertly designed
interventions may not achieve intended outcomes due to organisational readiness, competing priorities,
market conditions, or technical constraints that emerge during implementation. This uncertainty is not a
failure of planning—it is a reality of complex systems.
The question is not whether to accept this uncertainty, but how to allocate it fairly between client and
consultant. Under traditional models, TfNSW accepts all of this uncertainty while consultants face no
consequence for recommendations that fail to materialise as value.

2.2.          A Different Approach: Shared Risk, Aligned Returns
CBS Group proposes an alternative model structured around three foundational principles:
1. Shared conviction: Both parties agree there is genuine possibility of value in each initiative before work
   commences.
2. Co-investment: CBS Group assigns specialists on a retainer basis at 40-50% of market rates, with value,
   duration and value share agreed initiative-by-initiative.
3. Aligned returns: If value is delivered, we share in that value. If it is not, our returns are limited
   accordingly.
The following comparison illustrates how fundamentally this differs from traditional arrangements:

 Dimension             Traditional Model                                      Value-Based Model
 Payment trigger       Time expended or deliverables completed                Value delivered and validated
 Risk allocation       Client bears all value risk                            Risk shared between parties
 Consultant            None—full fees paid regardless of outcome              50-60% of market rate deferred, contingent on
 investment                                                                   value
 Success definition    Scope completion                                       Measurable improvement in outcomes
 Alignment of          Limited—consultant paid whether client benefits        Strong—consultant succeeds only when client
 interests             or not                                                 succeeds
 Validation            None                                                   Independent verification required
 requirement




                                                                                                     Page 3
 Dimension                    Traditional Model                                   Value-Based Model
 Knowledge transfer           Optional, often limited                             Essential—builds client capability to sustain
                                                                                  benefits

Risk and Reward Distribution
The model's value becomes clearer when examining how outcomes translate to returns:

 Scenario                            Traditional Model                       Value-Based Model
 Initiative delivers $2M in          Consultant receives full fee; TfNSW     Consultant receives reduced retainer plus 30% of
 benefits                            retains all benefit                     validated benefits; both parties benefit
 Initiative delivers no              Consultant receives full fee; TfNSW     Consultant receives only reduced retainer; both parties
 measurable benefit                  absorbs loss                            share disappointment
 Initiative partially succeeds       Consultant receives full fee; TfNSW     Consultant receives reduced retainer plus
 ($500K benefits)                    receives modest return                  proportionate share; returns match outcomes

2.3.          Why CBS Group Can Accept This Risk
Traditional consultants cannot easily adopt value-based models because they lack the tools to assess value
potential with confidence. CBS Group's position is different.
Our willingness to share value risk is underpinned by a proven toolkit developed through successful delivery
across major infrastructure projects including the Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney Harbour Tunnel, and M6
Stage 1:

 Capability                         Description                                                   Value Contribution
 CAPITAL Framework                  A sophisticated asset management and commercial               Validated methodology with
                                    framework that drives asset performance, industry             $180M+ savings on Western
                                    capability development, and whole-of-life cost certainty      Harbour Tunnel alone
 Lifecycle Cost Modelling           Comprehensive analysis influencing design and operational     Enables accurate benefit
                                    decisions                                                     quantification at initiative outset
 Operations Asset                   Facilitation and formalisation of operation and maintenance   Reduced waste in build, greater
 Management and                     concepts to ensure assets meet required needs with            performance in operate phase
 Maintenance Strategies             appropriate frameworks in place
 Co-Lab Framework                   A methodology that validates community service and safety     Asset rationalisation, reduced
                                    needs to challenge lazy precedents and ensures capex and      whole-of-life cost, improved
                                    opex savings through rationalised asset design                performance
 Performance Benchmarking           Comparison against international best practices               Identifies realistic improvement
                                                                                                  targets with evidence base
 Collaborative Teams                Application of ISO44001 to establish productive and           Reduced contract management
                                    enduring teams delivering on long-term contracts              friction, greater contract
                                                                                                  performance
 Asset Management                   A library of templates readily applied to long-term           Improved contractor performance
 Systems                            contracts, enabling TfNSW to transition to assurance-based    and reduced contract
                                    contract management sooner                                    management overhead

These capabilities do not eliminate uncertainty—they reduce it to a level where shared risk becomes
commercially viable.

2.4.          Partnership Foundation
This proposal builds upon CBS Group's established relationship with TfNSW, spanning over 20 years of
engagement with NSW transport infrastructure. Our current integration of CBS specialists within TfNSW has
demonstrated our critical thinking, pragmatic technical and commercial solutions, and ability to engage with
a wide variety of stakeholders.



                                                                                                       Page 4
To enhance the credibility of our value-based model, we have established a strategic alliance with ST
Engineering. Their role is to provide:
•    Independent validation of benefits claimed
•    Global benchmarking against international standards
•    Technology assessments based on worldwide deployments
Importantly, CBS Group remains the sole contracting party—we manage all coordination with ST Engineering,
maintaining a single interface for TfNSW throughout the engagement.

2.5.       Market Validation with ST Engineering
ST Engineering provides validation services that enhance the credibility of our value-based model without
adding complexity to the TfNSW relationship.
ST Engineering will provide a market lense on initiatives, enabling CBS Group analysis to be validated,
supported and benchmarked by a global industry leader with Australian and international experience in
delivery and operations of transport infrastructure.
The following principles govern this partnership:
•    CBS Group remains the sole contracting party with full accountability for all deliverables
•    ST Engineering provides advisory and validation services only, with no direct TfNSW interface
•    All ST Engineering services are included within CBS Group pricing
•    CBS Group interprets and applies all recommendations, maintaining delivery simplicity



3.         The Value-Based Engagement Model
3.1.       Core Principles
The value-based engagement model fundamentally restructures the consultant-client relationship around
three principles that address the limitations of traditional arrangements:
Principle 1: Shared Conviction
Before any initiative commences, both TfNSW and CBS Group must agree that a genuine possibility of value
exists. This is not a formality—it requires both parties to review the proposed intervention, examine the
evidence base, and commit resources only where there is mutual confidence in the potential for measurable
improvement.
Principle 2: Co-Investment
CBS Group assigns a team of appropriate specialists to agreed initiatives on a retainer basis. The value target,
task duration and value share percentage are agreed on an initiative-by-initiative basis. CBS Group's initial
fees are set at 40-50% of market rates, representing our investment in the partnership. This reduced fee is not
a discount—it reflects our willingness to accept a lower certain return in exchange for participation in value
upside.
Principle 3: Aligned Returns
If value is delivered and independently validated, CBS Group shares in that value through benefit-sharing
payments. If value does not materialise, our returns are limited to the reduced retainer. This structure
ensures that CBS Group's commercial success depends directly on TfNSW's outcomes.




                                                                                      Page 5
3.2.      Process Overview
The value-based pricing model operates as illustrated in the diagram and incorporating the following steps:
•   Client or CBS identifies a value-generating opportunity
•   CBS and TfNSW agree on scope, baseline costs, method of measurement (considering CATALYST 7™),
    start and end date, number of teams, and value share (with cap if required)
•   A Project Schedule is completed and executed by both parties
•   Project commences with monthly retainers for the agreed number of teams paid by TfNSW
•   Value share is paid once measurement outcomes are confirmed as documented in the Project Schedule




3.2.1.    Identification & Scoping
Either party may identify a potential value-generating opportunity. CBS Group will maintain an ongoing
pipeline of opportunities through its embedded team and strategic analysis.
For each identified opportunity, CBS Group and TfNSW will jointly:
•   Define the problem statement and anticipated outcome
•   Establish baseline costs or performance metrics
•   Agree the measurement methodology aligned with CATALYST 7™
•   Determine the number of teams and resource commitment required
•   Agree the start date, end date, and value measurement period
•   Negotiate the value share percentage and any applicable cap

3.2.2.    Agree Terms and Execute Schedule
The agreed terms will be documented in a Project Schedule, which forms a binding appendix to the Master
Agreement. Work on the initiative will not commence until both parties have executed the Project Schedule.

3.2.3.    Delivery
Upon execution of the Project Schedule:
•   Monthly retainers for the agreed number of teams commence
•   Baseline data is formally documented and certified


                                                                                   Page 6
•     The project team is mobilised in accordance with the agreed composition


3.2.4.        Realise Value
Upon completion of the initiative:
•     Value is measured in accordance with the agreed methodology
•     Benefits are validated through the applicable validation tier
•     Value share is calculated and paid as documented in the Project Schedule

3.3.          Project Schedule Requirements
Before work commences on any value-based initiative, both parties will agree and document the following in
a Project Schedule (Appendix A):
      1. Scope: The specific activities, deliverables and outcomes for the initiative
      2. Baseline: The current state costs or performance metrics against which value will be measured
      3. Measurement Method: The methodology for calculating value, aligned with the CATALYST 7™
         framework
      4. Duration: The start date, end date, and value measurement period
      5. Team Composition: The number and composition of teams assigned to the initiative
      6. Value Share: The percentage payable to CBS Group, including any applicable cap

3.4.          Retainer Framework
The value-based model operates through initiative-specific retainers, with the number of teams determined
for each Project Schedule based on the scope and complexity of the work.

3.4.1. Base Retainer Terms
    Element                          Standard Terms
    Retainer per team per month      $10,000 (excl. GST)
    Maximum specialists per team 3 (typically: senior lead, technical specialist, analyst)
    Payment timing                   In advance, by the 1st business day of each month
    Payment status                   Non-refundable, representing mutual commitment


3.4.2. Team Allocation
The number of teams assigned to each initiative will be agreed in the Project Schedule based on:
•     Scope and complexity of the initiative
•     Expected duration
•     Target value and delivery timeframes
•     Resource requirements identified during scoping
Multiple initiatives may run concurrently, each with its own team allocation and Project Schedule.




                                                                                      Page 7
3.4.4. Minimum Deliverables Within Retainer
Each monthly retainer includes:
•      Monthly performance dashboard and benefit tracking report
•      Access to CATALYST 7™ tools and templates for engaged projects
•      Monthly stakeholder briefing and progress review
•      Continuous monitoring of asset performance against established baselines

3.5.           Value Share Mechanism
Value share is calculated and paid on an initiative-by-initiative basis. For each value-generating opportunity,
CBS Group and TfNSW will execute a Project Schedule that documents the specific commercial terms
applicable to that initiative.
The value share payable for each initiative is calculated as follows:
Value Share = (Validated Benefits - Cumulative Retainer Paid) × Agreed Value Share Percentage
The value share is payable only after the measurement outcomes are confirmed through the validation
process documented in the Project Schedule.

3.6.           Commercial Framework
The commercial structure operationalises these principles through an integrated pricing arrangement:

    Element                   Value-Based Model
    Initial investment        40-50% of market rates
    Monthly retainer          $10,000 (non-refundable)
    Validation services       Included (via ST Engineering if applicable)
    Benefit share             Agreed share of validated benefits above retainer
    Payment frequency         Quarterly reconciliation based on validated achievements

This structure creates a partnership dynamic fundamentally different from traditional consulting. CBS Group
has a direct financial interest in ensuring that initiatives succeed—not merely that deliverables are
completed.




                                                                                         Page 8
3.7.             Validation Framework
3.7.1.           Validation Principles
The validation process is governed by the following principles to ensure both rigour and efficiency:
•     Transparency: All benefit calculations and supporting evidence are shared with TfNSW
•     Conservatism: Where uncertainty exists, benefit estimates err on the conservative side
•     Attribution: Only benefits demonstrably linked to CBS Group interventions are included
•     Timing: Benefits are recognised when realised, not when projected
•     Independence: ST Engineering provides objective assessment at all tiers

3.7.2.           Tiered Validation Approach
A robust validation framework is essential to the integrity of the value-based model. Both parties must have
confidence that claimed benefits are real, measurable and attributable to the engagement. We propose a
tiered validation approach that applies appropriate scrutiny proportionate to benefit magnitude.
The following table sets out the validation requirements at each tier (may be amended in the project schedule
by agreement):

    Value Range      Validation Method                       Review Period      ST Engineering Role         Cost Sharing
    < $100,000       Self-certification with pre agreed      3 business days    Review and endorse          CBS Group funds
                     documentation evidence
    $100,000 –       Self-certification with pre agreed      5 business days    Review and endorse          CBS Group funds
    $250,000         documentation evidence and peer
                     review
    $250,000 –       External review accepted by nominated   10 business days   Validate and                CBS Group funds
    $500,000         TfNSW Representative                                       benchmark
    > $500,000       As agreed in the Project Schedule       20 business days   As agreed in the            As agree in the
                                                                                Project Schedule            Project Schedule



3.7.3.           Benchmarking and Global Comparison
ST Engineering's involvement enables benefits to be contextualised against international standards. This
benchmarking serves two purposes: it validates that claimed improvements are genuine by reference to what
has been achieved elsewhere, and it identifies further opportunities by highlighting gaps between current
performance and global best practice.

4.2.         Initiative Selection and Prioritisation
The initiatives listed above represent opportunities identified through CBS Group's existing engagement with
TfNSW and analysis of strategic priorities. Not all initiatives need proceed simultaneously—the value-based
model allows for selective engagement based on TfNSW priorities and resource availability.
We propose the following approach to initiative selection:
      1. Joint prioritisation workshop: CBS Group and TfNSW jointly review the seed list and agree priority
         initiatives
      2. Value assessment: For each priority initiative, both parties review the value hypothesis and agree
         target outcomes
      3. Commercial agreement: Value target, duration, and value share percentage are agreed for each
         initiative
      4. Phased commencement: Initiatives commence in agreed sequence, allowing learnings to inform
         subsequent engagements

5.           Traditional Engagement Under Collaborative Terms
To enable engagement of our core and supplementary teams at a reduced set of rates, the proposed
amendments to the contract detailed at Appendix C Table 1 must be accepted.
To enable the value-based engagement model amendments to the contract detailed at Appendix C Table 2
must be accepted.
Upon acceptance of the terms at Appendix C, the schedule or rates at Appendix D can be applied.
These amendments are designed to be applied to TfNSW's Professional Services Contract (Version 4.0,
September 2024) as provided in Annexure A - Proposed Contract.




                                                                                                    Page 11
6.        Next Steps
6.1.      Engagement Process
We propose the following progression to establish the value-based partnership:

 Phase              Timing     Activities
 Framework          Weeks      Executive briefing on value-based model; ST Engineering validation
 Agreement          1-2        methodology review; selection of preferred commercial structure; initial
                               product prioritisation
 Commercial         Weeks      Contract amendments completion; governance structure establishment;
 Finalisation       3-4        validation criteria agreement; baseline methodology confirmation
 Partnership        Weeks      Team mobilisation including ST Engineering briefing; stakeholder
 Launch             5-6        communication; initial benchmarking study; commencement of first
                               initiative

6.2.      Our Commitment
CBS Group commits to fostering a genuine partnership with shared success, delivering validated and
measurable value, maintaining single-point accountability throughout the engagement, providing global
insights with local delivery, and creating alignment of interests that benefits both parties.
This partnership proposal is valid for 90 days and represents CBS Group's commitment to deliver world-
class, validated asset management solutions through a commercial model that ensures our success
depends on yours.

A.3. Conclusion
The value-based model protects TfNSW in downside scenarios while sharing upside with CBS Group when
initiatives succeed. This alignment of interests creates genuine partnership focused on outcomes rather than
activity.

## TfNSW AMSS RFP_CBS Group_Alternative Offer 3.pdf

*File: `Final PDFs/TfNSW AMSS RFP_CBS Group_Alternative Offer 3.pdf`*

TRANSPORT FOR NSW (TFNSW)

Alternative Proposal 3
CBS Value Based Products &
Solutions
Value Based Products & Solutions
Framework
RFP No. WS5364262133
Date 1st December 2025
1st December 2025


David Hanna
Strategic Sourcing Manager
Transport for NSW
Level 2, 7 Harvest Street
Macquarie Park NSW 2113
Subject: Alternate Offer 3 for Asset Management Services
Dear David,
On behalf of CBS Group, I am pleased to submit our alternate offer for the provision of Asset Management
services to Transport for NSW. This proposal responds to our discussions regarding access to specialist
products and is designed to operate in parallel with either our conforming base offer or Alternate 1 offer,
providing a supplemental engagement mechanism to meet TfNSW's asset management and value creation
needs over the term.
The offer provides TfNSW with access to a suite of CBS Group specialist products developed through our
extensive experience delivering asset management outcomes for major infrastructure. These products
represent proven methodologies, analytical tools and frameworks that have contributed to more than $1
billion in validated savings for TfNSW over the past three years.
Product Licensing
Each product may be acquired as a perpetual licence, providing TfNSW with ongoing access and the ability to
deploy the product independently across the organisation. This pathway suits products where TfNSW seeks
long-term capability uplift and internal ownership.
Product as a Service
Alternatively, products may be accessed on a service basis comprising an implementation fee and monthly
usage charges. This pathway provides flexibility for time-limited initiatives, pilot programs or where TfNSW
prefers operational expenditure over capital investment.
Collaborative Contract Terms
To accommodate the product licensing and usage mechanisms required, we have proposed a set of
amendments to the contract terms. These amendments address intellectual property provisions, licensing
arrangements and service-based commercial structures not contemplated in the standard terms.
Acceptance of these amendments enables the product framework to operate effectively alongside the
primary engagement.
This framework provides TfNSW with the flexibility to select the most appropriate commercial approach for
each product, enabling rapid deployment of proven tools to support asset management objectives.
We have enclosed a detailed proposal outlining a selection of current products, licensing options, proposed
contract amendments, and the value each product is designed to create. Thank you for the opportunity to
present this alternate offer. We look forward to discussing it with you further.
Sincerely,



Sarah Taylor,
Partner, Strategy and Technology
CBS Group Pty Ltd
TABLE OF CONTENTS
Executive summary ............................................................................................................................ 1

1.          Product Portfolio ..................................................................................................................... 1
1.1.        Selected products summary ..................................................................................................................................................................1
1.2.        Initiative Selection and Prioritisation..................................................................................................................................................3

2.          Next Steps .............................................................................................................................. 3
2.1.        Engagement Process ................................................................................................................................................................................3

Appendix A: Proposed Contract Amendments .................................................................................... 4
A.1. Amendments Common to Both Pathways ..............................................................................................................................................5
A.2. Amendments Specific to Perpetual Licence Pathway..........................................................................................................................6
A.3. Amendments Specific to Product as a Service (PaaS) Pathway .......................................................................................................7
Executive summary
Situation: Delivering effective asset management for complex infrastructure requires sophisticated tools,
methodologies and analytical frameworks. Developing these capabilities internally demands significant
investment in time, expertise and iterative refinement. Traditional consultant engagements provide access to
such tools on a project-by-project basis, but the intellectual property remains with the consultant—requiring
repeated engagement to access the same capabilities.
Complication: TfNSW faces growing pressure to deliver asset management outcomes efficiently while
building sustainable internal capability. Reliance on consultant-held tools creates dependency and limits
knowledge transfer. Procuring or developing equivalent products independently would require substantial
investment with uncertain outcomes. Standard contract terms do not contemplate product licensing or
service-based access models, creating a structural barrier to accessing proven tools through mechanisms
suited to their nature.
Response: CBS Group proposes an alternate engagement model designed to operate in parallel with either
the conforming base offer or Alternate 1 offer. This model provides access to our suite of specialist products
through a flexible dual-pathway commercial framework with three complementary mechanisms.
•   Product Licensing provides perpetual access to CBS Group products, enabling TfNSW to deploy proven
    tools independently across the organisation. This pathway supports long-term capability uplift and
    internal ownership of critical asset management methodologies.
•   Product as a Service provides access through an implementation fee and monthly usage charges. This
    pathway offers flexibility for time-limited initiatives, pilot programs or where operational expenditure is
    preferred over capital investment. It includes ongoing support and updates for the duration of the service
    arrangement.
•   Contract Amendments for Product Access details a set of proposed amendments to accommodate the
    licensing and usage mechanisms required. These amendments address intellectual property provisions,
    licensing arrangements and service-based commercial structures not contemplated in the standard
    terms, enabling the product framework to operate effectively alongside the primary engagement.
We bring a suite of proven asset management products, methodologies and analytical tools that have
delivered more than $1 billion in validated savings for TfNSW over the past three years. It is with this track
record that we offer these products with confidence in the value they will deliver.

                                                                                                                      Page 2
1.2.         Initiative Selection and Prioritisation
The initiatives listed above represent opportunities identified through CBS Group's existing engagement with
TfNSW and analysis of strategic priorities. Not all initiatives need proceed simultaneously—the value-based
model allows for selective engagement based on TfNSW priorities and resource availability.
We propose the following approach to initiative selection:
     1. Joint prioritisation workshop: CBS Group and TfNSW jointly review the seed list and agree priority
        initiatives
     2. Value assessment: For each priority initiative, both parties review the value hypothesis and agree
        target outcomes
     3. Commercial agreement: Value target, duration, and value share percentage are agreed for each
        initiative
     4. Phased commencement: Initiatives commence in agreed sequence, allowing learnings to inform
        subsequent engagements

2.           Next Steps
2.1.         Engagement Process
We propose the following progression to establish the products framework:

                                        initial product prioritisation

 Finalisation                           governance structure establishment; SLA and support arrangements
                                        confirmation
 Product Deployment         Weeks 5-8   Implementation of first priority product(s); user training and onboarding;
                                        integration with TfNSW systems as required; commencement of ongoing
                                        support arrangements

Appendix A: Proposed Contract Amendments
To implement this innovative framework within TfNSW's standard Professional Services Contract, we
propose the following amendments to accommodate the procurement pathways.
The proposed contract amendments required to enable the CBS Group Products Offer (Alternate 3) to
operate alongside the primary engagement. The Products Offer provides TfNSW with access to CBS Group's
specialist products through two commercial pathways:
    •     Perpetual Licence: One-time acquisition providing TfNSW with ongoing access and independent
          deployment capability across the organisation.
    •     Product as a Service (PaaS): Implementation fee plus recurring usage-based fees, providing
          flexibility for time-limited or pilot deployments with ongoing support and updates.

## TfNSW AMSS RFP_CBS Group_Conforming Offer.pdf

*File: `Final PDFs/TfNSW AMSS RFP_CBS Group_Conforming Offer.pdf`*

TRANSPORT FOR NSW

Proposal for Asset Management
Support Services
RFP No. WS5364262133
Date 1st December 2025
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

A PARTNERSHIP OF CERTAINTY, VALUE, AND COMMITMENT


On behalf of the entire CBS Group, I am delighted to present our proposal to continue     THE CERTAINTY OF ZERO TRANSITION RISK
our valued partnership with Transport for NSW (TfNSW).
                                                                                          Continuity is a powerful strategic advantage. Our proposal eliminates the significant
Over the past 18 months, we have been privileged to work alongside your Asset
                                                                                          risks, costs, and delays associated with onboarding a new provider. We offer TfNSW
Management team on some of the state’s most significant infrastructure programs.
                                                                                          the certainty of immediate, uninterrupted productivity from Day One by proposing a
The trust you have placed in us is a responsibility we take very seriously.
                                                                                          core team of seven senior specialists, most of whom who are already delivering for
This proposal is our commitment to repaying that trust by delivering what we believe      you. This team, with over 175 years of combined experience, possesses an invaluable
no other partner can: the certainty of proven performance, the security of zero           understanding of your assets, governance, and priorities. By choosing CBS Group,
transition risk, and a genuine partnership dedicated to delivering value to the           you avoid a typical 7-12 month learning curve and the associated productivity dip.
people of NSW.
                                                                                          THE CERTAINTY OF A GENUINE PARTNERSHIP AND COMMITMENT
Our proposal is built on a foundation of certainty. In a landscape often defined by
complexity and unpredictability, CBS Group offers a proven, low-risk path to              As a 100% Australian-owned SME, our success is intrinsically linked to the prosperity
achieving TfNSW’s strategic asset management objectives.                                  of NSW. We offer an agile, responsive service model that provides direct access to
                                                                                          senior leadership, ensuring greater efficiency and value. Our commitment extends

CBS Group's unique market position ensures our recommendations serve only the               these competing tensions. With no downstream delivery contracts or transaction fees
State's best interests, free from the conflicts inherent in traditional advisory options.   influencing our advice, we challenge established norms and drive innovation aligned

In procuring strategic asset management advice, TfNSW must navigate three distinct
risks:                                                                                      With CBS, TfNSW has absolute certainty: impartial advice, rigorous analysis, and a
                                                                                            singular focus on your success.
       Analytical capability without operational insight — rigorous analysis
        undermined by operations-phase blind spots, defaults to "lazy precedent,"
        and innovation constrained by professional indemnity exposure.

       Operational experience compromised by commercial interest — delivery
        knowledge shaped to suit the adviser's own capabilities or downstream
        ambitions, creating conflicts that undermine procurement integrity.

Confirmation of Conditions of Participation
We are deeply grateful for the trust Transport for NSW (TfNSW) has placed in CBS Group over the past 18 months.
It has been a privilege to support your critical infrastructure programs – the Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney
Harbour Tunnel, and M6 Stage 1 – and to work alongside your talented Asset Management team.
We are pleased to confirm that CBS Group meets the Conditions of Participation outlined in Section 2.1 of the
RFP. We have demonstrated experience in two or more named transactions in multiple areas of expertise listed.
Our recent and direct experience on the Western Harbour Tunnel (WHT), Sydney Harbour Tunnel (SHT) and M6
Stage 1 (M6) projects provides clear and irrefutable evidence of our capability.
The table below directly maps our experience against the specific areas of expertise required by TfNSW.

 Required Area of Expertise              CBS Group's Demonstrated Experience & Relevant Project(s)
 (RFP 2.1)
 Asset Management input to systems       Together with TfNSW, we developed the video-only tolling model and WOL costings for
 and tolling, evaluation and WOL         the WHT, and have provided ongoing systems and tolling advice for the SHT and M6.
 costing for road or rail tunnels        Projects: WHT, SHT, M6
 Asset Due Diligence                     Our team led the technical and commercial due diligence during the tender evaluation
                                         and contract mobilisation phases for the WHT, SHT and M6 Asset Manager
                                         engagements. Projects: WHT, SHT, M6
 Pre-Tender Estimates for 30 plus year   We developed the pre-tender estimates and the entire 30-year fixed-fee asset
 Asset Management or PPP contracts in    management model (the CAPITAL Framework) that underpins the WHT, SHT and M6
 road or rail                            contracts. Projects: WHT, SHT, M6
 Lifecycle Cost Modelling for tunnels    Our team, led by a specialist in WOL cost and reliability modelling, developed the
                                         detailed lifecycle cost models for the WHT, SHT and M6 tunnels. Projects: WHT, SHT, M6

 Asset Management contract               We designed and managed the end-to-end asset management contract procurement
 procurement processes                   process for the WHT, SHT and M6, from market engagement to contract award. Projects:
                                         WHT, SHT, M6
 Asset Commissioning                     Our team has extensive experience in asset commissioning, including developing
                                         operational readiness plans and managing the transition from construction to operation
                                         for major tunnel projects.
                                         Projects: WestConnex, M4, M5, M8
 Asset Class Strategies                  We have provided strategic advice to TfNSW on asset class strategies for the entire
                                         motorway network, helping to shape the long-term approach to asset management.
                                         Projects: TfNSW Motorway Network

 Ethics
 The Respondent warrants that it:
  has read and agrees to abide by the contents of the Statement of Business Ethics.
  has not entered into any improper, illegal, collusive or anti-competitive arrangements with any competitor.
  has not directly or indirectly approached any representative of the Agency (other than the RFP Manager) to lobby or solicit
   information in relation to the RFP.
  has not attempted to influence, or provide any form of personal inducement, reward or benefit to any representative of the
   Agency.
  has not been found by the Independent Commission Against Corruption (and nor have any Personnel) to have engaged in
   corrupt conduct as defined by the Independent Commission Against Corruption Act 1988 (NSW).
  has not engaged in any actions (and nor have any Personnel) regardless of whether or not such is in relation to this RFP, that
   might reflect badly on the Agency or the State of New South Wales; and
  has made reasonable inquiries of its Personnel involved in this RFP in relation to the ethical issues raised in this section.
 Agreed

 Basis of exclusion from RFP
 In addition to the above, the Respondent/s warrants that it:
  is not insolvent or subject to bankruptcy proceedings.
  has not failed to pay taxes.
  has not been convicted and/or is subject to proceedings of an offence punishable by imprisonment for a term of 2 years or
   more, or by a fine of $200,000 or more; or
  has not been found guilty and/or is subject to any investigation of professional misconduct or unprofessional conduct in a
   jurisdiction in Australia.
 Agreed

3.            Contract, insurances, financial
 Proposed Contract will be provided as an annexure to this document (Annexure A)
 Noted


 The Agency’s minimum insurance requirements are:
  Product Liability - $20m for each claim
  Public Liability - $20m for each claim
  Professional Indemnity - $10m for each claim
  Workers Compensation - as required by law
 Note: that insurance must be held with one of the following:
  An Australian registered insurance company that is approved by the Australian Prudential Regulatory Authority (APRA) to
     conduct general insurance business in Australia
  Lloyds Underwriters.
 Respondent to provide certificates of currency for the insurances listed above.
 Copies of the following CBS insurance certificates of currency are provided in Attachment A Insurance Certificates:
  Product and Public Liability - $20m for each claim
  Professional Indemnity - $10m for each claim
  Workers Compensation - as required by law
 All policies are held with APRA-approved insurers.

   4.          Resource capability and availability
   CBS Group offers TfNSW the team you already know, trust and rely on.
   Our proposed Core Team of seven senior specialists – Jeff Dusting, Jim Ellwood, Nick May, Lucas Rathmanner,
   George Khoury, David Harper and Kate Heath – are generally not new faces requiring introduction; they are the
   proven professionals currently delivering exceptional results on your Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney Harbour
   Tunnel and M6 Stage 1 projects.
   By selecting CBS Group, TfNSW eliminates the single greatest risk in any professional services engagement: the
   uncertainty of working with an unfamiliar team using untested methodologies. Instead, you gain immediate
   operational readiness and continue a partnership that is already delivering measurable value.
   Our team's experience averages 25+ years per person, totalling 175+ years of combined expertise in asset
   management, tolling, ITS, operations and lifecycle planning. More importantly, we know your assets, your
   governance frameworks, your stakeholders and your strategic priorities. This is a proposal for future performance
   and a commitment to continue the excellent performance you are benefitting from today.

   4.1.        Resource capability and availability
Please complete Annexure B outlining resource capability and availability.




   Response synopsis:
   Our Core Team is the team you know – led by Jeff and Jim. Nick, George, Lucas and David
   will continue the specialist expertise that have delivered success over the last two
   years. With no learning curve, they will add value from Day 1. The addition of Kate Heath
   brings timely and relevant market and transaction knowledge to the team, as she
   leverages expertise gained in senior roles at Downer, Ventia, Broadspectrum and
   Cushman Wakefield.
   To provide TfNSW with ultimate flexibility, this Core Team is supplemented by named
   specialists and a strategic partnership with Resolve Group. TfNSW can access these
   resources for temporary expertise or surge capacity to meet expedited timelines.
   CBS Group has completed Annexure B with our resource capability and availability,
   which is located with our Section 9 Pricing response. Below we provide more detail on
   the capability and coverage of our team against TfNSW’s service requirements and
   outcomes for the AMSS contract.



   4.1.1.      Core Team: Seven specialists with 175+ years’ combined experience
   Our lean 7.0 FTE structure comprehensively covers all RFP requirements with deep specialisation:
       Jeff Dusting (Project Director) – ISO 55001 CFAM, CAPITAL Framework author, 25+ years’ experience
       Jim Ellwood (Principal Asset Advisor) – ISO 55001 credentials, 25+ years’ asset management experience
       Nick May (Tolling & Systems Lead) – Video-only tolling pioneer, 25+ years’ ITS expertise
       George Khoury, David Harper, Lucas Rathmanner (Specialist Technical Advisors) – O&M, financial modelling,
        systems engineering
       Kate Heath (Principal Commercial and Transaction Advisor) – contract and project management, commercial
        negotiations, data and document management

Immediate productivity and seamless transition
Most Core Team members are currently working on TfNSW programs. We know TfNSW assets, governance
frameworks, stakeholders, systems and strategic priorities. This enables 100% Day 1 productivity.

Knowledge transfer and capability building
CBS Group is committed to continuing our collaborative deliver model with TfNSW, which grows capability in
TfNSW and embeds knowledge transfer between and within both our organisations. TfNSW staff work alongside
CBS consultants, building lasting capability that reduces long-term reliance on external advisors. More detail on
our approach is included in Section 4.3.

4.1.2.         A trusted partner for TfNSW across the duration of the contract
To further assure you that you can rely on our capability and track record, we commit to maintain our nominated
Core Team through all delivery phases (Initial Period, Option Periods, Transition-Out). This commitment is
established in contractual protections. Resource allocation remains flexible to TfNSW’s needs:
    Delivery phase           Allocation of team
    Initial Period           7.0 FTE baseline – All seven Core Team members engaged
    (First 6 months)         Focus: Immediate delivery of seven Initial Period deliverables, establishment of governance frameworks,
                             commencement of ongoing service delivery
                             Discretionary Resources: Engaged as needed for surge capacity (e.g. Kate Heath for complex
                             commercial negotiations, Adam Noonan for environmental assessments)
    Option Periods           6.0-8.0 FTE flexible – Core Team scaled based on TfNSW demand
                             Focus: Ongoing delivery across 6 RFP service areas, continuous improvement, knowledge transfer
                             Discretionary Resources: Engaged as needed for specialised expertise or surge capacity
    Transition-Out Period    7.0-8.0 FTE – Full Core Team plus transition support
                             Focus: Comprehensive handover, knowledge transfer, stakeholder relationship transition,
                             documentation finalisation
                             Discretionary Resources: Engaged to ensure seamless transition

 The $1.049B in validated savings was achieved through close collaboration with Transport for NSW's Asset Management team. This outcome reflects
the strength of our partnership and TfNSW's strategic vision and commitment to value optimisation.

   TRANSPORT FOR NSW
   Proposal for Asset Management Support Services



   4.1.3.     Supplementary Resources
   We offer TfNSW the ability to engage additional support more rapidly, and in a commercially simpler way, by
   drawing on supplementary team resources not included in our fixed price. These additional resources are
   available to provide TfNSW with a more extensive breadth of subject matter expertise and capability which we
   have mapped to additional activities that may be required over the course of the contract term. Key supplementary
   resources are included in the organisation chart in Section 4.2.1. We have included the Supplementary Team with
   our resource activity mapping, their bio’s and full CV’s in Attachment C.


   4.2.       Team structure and subcontractor relationships
Provide details of the proposed structure of your team and expected relationships with proposed subcontractors (if
applicable) for each delivery phase, including:
1. the key resources identified in this Returnable Schedule organised into a delivery hierarchy
2. the roles and responsibilities of each resource, including the teams they would be expected to manage.



   Response synopsis:
   CBS Group's offer is for our dedicated Core Team, organised into a three-tiered
   hierarchy to deliver all core RFP requirements with proven expertise and 100% Day One
   productivity.
   Our key resources are specialists, and each is cross skilled across many AMSS required
   skill sets to provide technical, operational and commercial advisory. This brings a high
   level of quality and productivity, and reduces inefficiencies that often arise in handovers
   across disciplines.
   Our nominated team provides all required skills and services to rapidly realise TfNSW
   outcomes with no proposed sub contractors.
   We propose to maintain this structure and leadership team through all delivery phases,
   and may add team leads at level two should supplementary support be engaged to
   support additional tasks.



   4.2.1.     Organisation and delivery hierarchy: Certainty with a cohesive, proven team
   CBS Group proposes a collaborative, integrated Core Team structure to deliver seamless service across all
   RFP requirements. The three tiers of our structure provide clear accountability and governance; enable
   autonomous, expert-led execution; and make deep specialist expertise available across all service areas.
   The team is a lean, highly experienced group of senior experts. TfNSW gains direct access to the highest levels
   of strategic advice and technical expertise. The team is led by Jeff Dusting as the primary point of contact and
   engagement lead. He will assure the overall quality and ongoing value in the delivery of the Services.

   Organisational design
   Our structure is flat and collaborative, reflecting our ‘one team’ approach. This ensures that TfNSW has a clear
   and direct line of communication to the specific subject matter expert best placed to answer their query, while
   maintaining a single point of accountability for the overall engagement.
   No team member leads any other internal or external team. All team leadership activity undertaken by any
   member of the Core Team is directed solely to the TfNSW teams and activities.

TRANSPORT FOR NSW
Proposal for Asset Management Support Services




4.2.2.         Roles and responsibilities
Our people: Your proven team
CBS Group proposes the exact team of senior experts who established and delivered the Western Harbour Tunnel,
Sydney Harbour Tunnel and M6 Stage 1 asset management contracts. These are the professionals who've been
integral to your program's success from inception.
Our team includes:
      The principal architects of the AM Contract Model (CAPITAL Framework) as applied to WHT, SHT and M6
      Key personnel who led your tender evaluations and developed operational concepts
      Technical specialists who engineered systems for these specific assets

Tier 1: Strategic leadership and accountability
Jeff Dusting – Strategic oversight and accountability
Jeff serves as the single point of accountability to TfNSW, providing strategic oversight, executive relationship
management and ultimate quality assurance across all deliverables.
Unique value: As the author of the CAPITAL Framework that has delivered $1.049B1 in validated savings for TfNSW,
Jeff brings exceptional strategic asset management expertise.
    Key responsibilities                                           Qualifications & experience
    Single point of accountability to TfNSW for all deliverables   MBA (Distinction), MSc (Test & Eval.), BEng (Aerospace, Hons)
    Strategic oversight and quality assurance                      Certified Fellow of Asset Management (CFAM), ISO 55001
    Executive relationship management with TfNSW leadership        Fellow Engineers Australia (FIEAust), CEng
    Final approval authority on all deliverables                   25+ years’ strategic asset management experience
    Escalation point for unresolved issues                         Author of CAPITAL Framework (30-year proven methodology)
    CAPITAL Framework application and strategic asset              Current engagement: WHT, SHT, M6 strategic advisor
    management guidance




                                                                                                                              Page 10
TRANSPORT FOR NSW
Proposal for Asset Management Support Services



Tier 2: Service stream leadership
Two empowered delivery leads manage day-to-day execution in distinct service streams, reporting directly to Jeff
Dusting. These are senior experts who make operational decisions autonomously within agreed frameworks,
ensuring responsive and agile service delivery.
Jim Ellwood – Technical Services Lead
Jim leads Technical Services, encompassing asset management planning, lifecycle cost modelling, due diligence
and technical assurance.
Unique value: Jim's deep experience in the application of ISO 55001 asset management principles ensures
rigorous, defensible technical analysis aligned with international standards.
 Key responsibilities                                         Qualifications & experience
 Asset management plans and strategies                        MEng (Maintenance & Reliability), BEng (Aerospace, Hons),
 Lifecycle cost modelling and WOL analysis                    MIEAust
 Technical due diligence and assurance                        ISO 55001 application experience in complex projects
 Risk transfer optimisation                                   25+ years’ asset management and lifecycle cost modelling
 Performance-based contracting frameworks                     expertise
 Asset commissioning support                                  Current engagement: WHT, SHT, M6 technical lead
 Technical evaluation of Asset Manager proposals

Nick May – Tolling & Systems Specialist Lead
Nick leads the specialist technical resources for tolling systems and technology, working closely with Jim Ellwood
and others to optimise delivery of services.
Unique value: Nick is the only expert in video-only tolling in the Australian market, providing TfNSW with access to
specialised expertise not available elsewhere.
 Key responsibilities                                         Qualifications & experience
 Tolling systems and technology expertise                     MEng (Hons) Mechanical Engineering, 1st Class
 Video-only tolling (unique expertise in Australian market)   Business and Enterprise Architecture
 Systems architecture and integration                         27 years’ tolling and ITS experience
 Tolling procurement and evaluation                           Current engagement: WHT tolling systems advisor
 Tolling technology due diligence


Tier 3: Specialist advisors
Four specialist advisors provide domain expertise across technical stream including tolling, operations, WOL
analysis and ITS systems, as well as contracting and commercial management. These specialists are assigned to
specific workstreams as needed, receiving technical direction from the service stream leads, while remaining
accountable to Jeff Dusting as the Project Director. This is in keeping with our non-hierarchical organisational
approach that supports collaboration, integration and innovation while maintaining accountability and
productivity.
Lucas Rathmanner – ITS & Systems Engineering Specialist
Unique value: Lucas's expertise in ITS, tolling and control systems across many industries delivers WOL value
across complex transport infrastructure. His proven track record includes developing technical and systems
engineering specifications for the unique WHT tolling solution, KPI and performance modelling and leading the
development of a pragmatic solution and specification for the SHT OMCS.
 Key responsibilities                                         Qualifications & experience
 Operations Management and Control Systems (OMCS)             17+ years’ ITS/control systems engineering experience
 Tolling systems and integration                              Strategic asset lifecycle planning expertise
 Asset lifecycle planning                                     Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical), UTS
 Systems engineering and value delivery                       Current engagement: SHT OMCS and WHT tolling, TfNSW

9.2.           Transition-out services
TfNSW may select one (or both) of the following two transition out options:
Option 1: A Traditional transition-out where nominated successors are identified and a transition of knowledge
and support is scheduled over 4 weeks. Costs associated with this option are limited to the CBS Transition
Manager who is charged with:
      confirming the scope of knowledge and skills to be transitioned
      working with TfNSW to confirm the transition recipients for any ongoing works and associated document
       storage locations
      development of a transition guide capturing the location of artefacts and status of works in progress
Option 2: As detailed Alternate Offer 3, creation of an instance of CBS Continuity that provides an enduring
chatbot so that future TfNSW can continue to engage with the CBS team knowledge as established at the point of
transition out.
Pricing for both options is provided in the following table, noting that in the event that more than one CBS staff
member is retained, Transition Out is provided at a 50% cost reduction to the rates shown below.

9.5.       Discounts and rebates
Please provide details of any discounts or rebates that apply to the Fees and the circumstances under which
they apply

CBS Group will apply a discount of 2.5% to the escalated rates applied to any new purchase orders of greater than
$1m issued. Purchase orders below this threshold do not attract this discount, and the initial fixed fee engagement
does not attract this discount.
Discounts will also apply under the same conditions in the event that Alternate Offer 1 is selected.

9.6.       Pricing Assumptions
Please provide any assumptions that have been made by the Respondent in relation to any information
provided in response to this Returnable Schedule



All travel for Melbourne based resources is incorporated into the rates and assuming prior travel requirements and
frequencies are sustained. Any material change in travel requirements will result in an adjustment to the rate or a
request for travel costs.

The Upper Limiting Fee (ULF) is calculated on the basis of the 6 months from January to June 2026, percentages
shown are a percentage of the available work days after considering TfNSW shutdown and public holidays.

The ULF does not include our recommended contingency of 10-15% to provide flexibility during delivery and
possible peak activity requirements.
