Executive summary
Situation: Traditional models of consultant engagement leave all value risk with the client. Consultants are
paid for their time and deliverables regardless of whether their recommendations generate tangible benefits.
This creates a fundamental misalignment of incentives—the consultant's commercial success is
disconnected from the client's actual outcomes. Similarly, standard contract terms allocate risks to the
consultant that come at a cost, yet provide no corresponding benefit to the client when those risks do not
materialise.
Complication: Value in complex infrastructure engagements is inherently uncertain. Even well-conceived
initiatives may not deliver anticipated benefits due to implementation challenges, changing circumstances or
unforeseen constraints. Under traditional arrangements, TfNSW bears this uncertainty entirely while
consultants face no consequence for recommendations that fail to materialise as value. Meanwhile, contract
terms that transfer risk to consultants simply result in higher fees to cover contingencies that may never
eventuate—cost without benefit.
Response: CBSE proposes an alternate engagement model comprising a flexible dual-pathway commercial
framework with three complementary mechanisms.
1. Method 1 - Traditional Time-Based Engagement provides access to the full depth of expertise available
   from CBS Group and Egis through conventional fee arrangements for engagements with clearly defined
   scope on Collaborative Contract Terms. It provides for engagement of our core and supplementary
   teams on amended commercial terms. TfNSW acceptance of these terms allows a reduction in CBSE
   costs and risk premium, enabling reduced fees. This creates a direct exchange: TfNSW accepts
   manageable risks in return for tangible cost savings.
2. Method 2 - Value Based Pricing structures our compensation around shared risk and aligned returns,
   operating on three foundational principles: shared conviction (both parties agree there is genuine
   possibility of value before work commences), co-investment (CBSE assigns appropriate specialists on a
   fixed retainer basis, with value share agreed initiative-by-initiative), and aligned returns (if value is
   delivered, we share in it; if not, our returns are limited accordingly).
We bring a suite of proven asset management products, methodologies and analytical tools that have
delivered more than $1 billion in validated savings for TfNSW over the past three years. Combined with the
global capabilities of Egis, it is with this capability and track record that we are able to accept shared risk with
confidence.
Mechanism 1 (Traditional Time-Based Engagement) and Mechanism 2 (Collaborative Contract Terms) are
detailed at Section 4 of this Alternate Proposal. The remainder of this document explains the value and
operation of Mechanism 3 (Value Based Pricing).
We look forward to working through
our seed list of initiatives and the
necessary commercial
amendments to rapidly enable this
model and start driving real value to
support the Asset Financial
Sustainability (AFS) targets set for
the organisation.
1.          Dual engagement pathway framework
1.1.        Framework structure
This alternative framework is built on two distinct and complementary engagement pathways, allowing
TfNSW to select the most suitable approach for any given initiative.
Method 1: Traditional, Time-Based Engagement
This pathway follows a conventional consultancy model. Services are provided at market rates for a defined
scope of work. This option is suitable for projects where the scope and deliverables are clearly defined from
the outset and where a traditional risk allocation is preferred. Services are provided at market rates for a
defined scope of work. Work orders do not include value share provisions.
Method 2: Shared-Risk, Value-Based Engagement
This pathway is founded on a partnership model. CBSE accepts a significantly reduced monthly retainer,
representing a substantial investment from our side. In return, we participate in the value we help create
through a pre-agreed sharing mechanism on validated benefits. This model ensures that both organisations
share in the risks and rewards, creating a strong alignment of interests.

1.2.        Selection Criteria
The choice between Method 1 and Method 2 would be guided by the nature of the project.
    Method 1 is the preferred choice for more straightforward engagements. This includes defined-scope
     studies, technical investigations, or when seeking short-term expert advice. It is also appropriate for
     engagements where a more conservative, traditional risk allocation is desired
    Method 2 is particularly well-suited for initiatives with the potential for significant value creation, such as
     long-term transformation programs or projects that require innovative solutions. It is most effective in
     situations where a deep, collaborative partnership and fully aligned incentives are critical to success.

1.3.        Flexibility and Combination
The framework is designed for flexibility, allowing TfNSW to:
    Employ different options for different projects concurrently
    Transition between the two options with an agreed-upon notice period
    Combine both options within a single large-scale program
    Initiate with a pilot of Method 1 before committing to a broader rollout
2.           The Case for a New Engagement Model (Method 2)
2.1.         The Problem with Traditional Consulting
The conventional consulting model contains a structural flaw that limits its effectiveness for infrastructure
agencies. Under traditional fee-for-service arrangements, consultants are compensated for effort expended
rather than value delivered.
This misalignment creates predictable consequences:

 Characteristic           Traditional Model                              Consequence for TfNSW
 Payment basis            Time and materials or fixed fee for            Payment made regardless of whether
                          deliverables                                   recommendations generate value
 Risk allocation          Client bears all implementation and            TfNSW absorbs losses if initiatives fail to deliver
                          realisation risk                               anticipated benefits
 Consultant incentive     Maximise billable hours or scope               Focus shifts to engagement extension rather than
                                                                         rapid value delivery
 Success measurement      Deliverable completion                         Reports and recommendations may sit
                                                                         unimplemented
 Knowledge transfer       Limited incentive to build client capability   Dependency on consultant for ongoing support

Infrastructure asset management initiatives involve inherent uncertainty. Even expertly designed
interventions may not achieve intended outcomes due to organisational readiness, competing priorities,
market conditions, or technical constraints that emerge during implementation. This uncertainty is not a
failure of planning—it is a reality of complex systems.
The question is not whether to accept this uncertainty, but how to allocate it fairly between client and
consultant. Under traditional models, TfNSW accepts all of this uncertainty while consultants face no
consequence for recommendations that fail to materialise as value.

2.2.         A Different Approach: Shared Risk, Aligned Returns
CBS Group proposes an alternative model structured around three foundational principles:
     1. Shared conviction: Both parties agree there is genuine possibility of value in each initiative before
        work commences.
     2. Co-investment: CBSE JV assigns specialists on a retainer basis at 40-50% of market rates, with
        value, duration and value share agreed initiative-by-initiative.
     3. Aligned returns: If value is delivered, we share in that value. If it is not, our returns are limited
        accordingly.
The following comparison illustrates how fundamentally this differs from traditional arrangements:

 Dimension                Traditional Model                              Value-Based Model
 Payment trigger          Time expended or deliverables completed        Value delivered and validated
 Risk allocation          Client bears all value risk                    Risk shared between parties
 Consultant investment    None—full fees paid regardless of outcome      50-60% of market rate deferred, contingent on
                                                                         value
 Success definition       Scope completion                               Measurable improvement in outcomes
 Alignment of interests   Limited—consultant paid whether client         Strong—consultant succeeds only when client
                          benefits or not                                succeeds
 Validation requirement   None                                           Independent verification required
 Knowledge transfer       Optional, often limited                        Essential—builds client capability to sustain
                                                                         benefits
Risk and Reward Distribution
The model's value becomes clearer when examining how outcomes translate to returns:

 Scenario                     Traditional Model                           Value-Based Model
 Initiative delivers $2M in   Consultant receives full fee; TfNSW         Consultant receives reduced retainer plus 30% of
 benefits                     retains all benefit                         validated benefits; both parties benefit
 Initiative delivers no       Consultant receives full fee; TfNSW         Consultant receives only reduced retainer; both parties
 measurable benefit           absorbs loss                                share disappointment
 Initiative partially         Consultant receives full fee; TfNSW         Consultant receives reduced retainer plus
 succeeds ($500K benefits)    receives modest return                      proportionate share; returns match outcomes



2.3.          Why CBSE JV Can Accept This Risk
Traditional consultants cannot easily adopt value-based models because they lack the tools to assess value
potential with confidence. CBSE JV's position is different.
Our willingness to share value risk is underpinned by a proven toolkit developed through successful delivery
across major infrastructure projects including the Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney Harbour Tunnel, and M6
Stage 1:

 Capability                   Description                                                      Value Contribution
 CAPITAL Framework            A sophisticated asset management and commercial                  Validated methodology with
                              framework that drives asset performance, industry capability     $180M+ savings on Western
                              development, and whole-of-life cost certainty                    Harbour Tunnel alone
 Lifecycle Cost Modelling     Comprehensive analysis influencing design and operational        Enables accurate benefit
                              decisions                                                        quantification at initiative outset
 Operations Asset             Facilitation and formalisation of operation and maintenance      Reduced waste in build, greater
 Management and               concepts to ensure assets meet required needs with               performance in operate phase
 Maintenance Strategies       appropriate frameworks in place
 Co-Lab Framework             A methodology that validates community service and safety        Asset rationalisation, reduced
                              needs to challenge lazy precedents and ensures capex and         whole-of-life cost, improved
                              opex savings through rationalised asset design                   performance
 Performance                  Comparison against international best practices                  Identifies realistic improvement
 Benchmarking                                                                                  targets with evidence base
 Collaborative Teams          Application of ISO44001 to establish productive and enduring     Reduced contract management
                              teams delivering on long-term contracts                          friction, greater contract
                                                                                               performance
 Asset Management             A library of templates readily applied to long-term contracts,   Improved contractor performance
 Systems                      enabling TfNSW to transition to assurance-based contract         and reduced contract
                              management sooner                                                management overhead



These capabilities do not eliminate uncertainty—they reduce it to a level where shared risk becomes
commercially viable.
2.4.      Partnership Foundation
This proposal builds upon CBS Group's established relationship with TfNSW, spanning over 20 years of
engagement with NSW transport infrastructure. Our current integration of CBS specialists within TfNSW has
demonstrated our critical thinking, pragmatic technical and commercial solutions, and ability to engage with
a wide variety of stakeholders.
To enhance the credibility of our value-based model, we have established a strategic alliance with ST
Engineering. Their role is to provide:
   Independent validation of benefits claimed
   Global benchmarking against international standards
   Technology assessments based on worldwide deployments
Importantly, CBSE JV remains the sole contracting party—we manage all coordination with ST Engineering,
maintaining a single interface for TfNSW throughout the engagement.
3.         The Value-Based Engagement Model
3.1.       Core principles
The value-based engagement model fundamentally restructures the consultant-client relationship around
three principles that address the limitations of traditional arrangements:

3.1.1.     Principle 1: Shared Conviction
Before any initiative commences, both TfNSW and CBSE JV must agree that a genuine possibility of value
exists. This is not a formality—it requires both parties to review the proposed intervention, examine the
evidence base, and commit resources only where there is mutual confidence in the potential for measurable
improvement.

3.1.2.     Principle 2: Co-Investment
 CBSE JV assigns a team of appropriate specialists to agreed initiatives on a retainer basis. The value target,
task duration and value share percentage are agreed on an initiative-by-initiative basis. CBSE JV's initial fees
are set at 40-50% of market rates, representing our investment in the partnership. This reduced fee is not a
discount—it reflects our willingness to accept a lower certain return in exchange for participation in value
upside.

3.1.3.     Principle 3: Aligned Returns
If value is delivered and independently validated, CBSE JV shares in that value through benefit-sharing
payments. If value does not materialise, our returns are limited to the reduced retainer. This structure
ensures that CBSE JV's commercial success depends directly on TfNSW's outcomes.

3.2.       Process overview
The value-based pricing model operates as illustrated in the diagram and incorporating the following steps:
    Client or CBSE identifies a value-generating opportunity
    CBSE and TfNSW agree on scope, baseline costs, method of measurement (considering CATALYST 7™),
     start and end date, number of teams, and value share (with cap if required)
    A Project Schedule is completed and executed by both parties
    Project commences with monthly retainers for the agreed number of teams paid by TfNSW
    Value share is paid once measurement outcomes are confirmed as documented in the Project Schedule
3.2.1.         Identification & Scoping
Either party may identify a potential value-generating opportunity. CBSE JV will maintain an ongoing pipeline
of opportunities through its embedded team and strategic analysis.
For each identified opportunity, CBSE JV and TfNSW will jointly:
      Define the problem statement and anticipated outcome
      Establish baseline costs or performance metrics
      Agree the measurement methodology aligned with CATALYST 7™1
      Determine the number of teams and resource commitment required
      Agree the start date, end date, and value measurement period
      Negotiate the value share percentage and any applicable cap

3.2.2.         Agree Terms and Execute Schedule
The agreed terms will be documented in a Project Schedule, which forms a binding appendix to the Master
Agreement. Work on the initiative will not commence until both parties have executed the Project Schedule.

3.2.3.         Delivery
Upon execution of the Project Schedule:
      Monthly retainers for the agreed number of teams commence
      Baseline data is formally documented and certified
      The project team is mobilised in accordance with the agreed composition

3.2.4.         Realise Value
Upon completion of the initiative:
      Value is measured in accordance with the agreed methodology
      Benefits are validated through the applicable validation tier
      Value share is calculated and paid as documented in the Project Schedule

3.3.           Project Schedule Requirements
Before work commences on any value-based initiative, both parties will agree and document the following in
a Project Schedule (Appendix A):
       1. Scope: The specific activities, deliverables and outcomes for the initiative
       2. Baseline: The current state costs or performance metrics against which value will be measured
       3. Measurement Method: The methodology for calculating value, aligned with the CATALYST 7™
          framework
       4. Duration: The start date, end date, and value measurement period
       5. Team Composition: The number and composition of teams assigned to the initiative
       6. Value Share: The percentage payable to CBSE JV, including any applicable cap

3.4.           Retainer Framework
The value-based model operates through initiative-specific retainers, with the number of teams determined
for each Project Schedule based on the scope and complexity of the work.



1
    See Appendix E: CATALYST 7™ Measurement Framework for detailed methodology.
3.4.1. Base Retainer Terms
    Element                             Standard Terms
    Retainer per team per month         $10,000 (excl. GST)
    Maximum specialists per team        3 (typically: senior lead, technical specialist, analyst)
    Payment timing                      In advance, by the 1st business day of each month
    Payment status                      Non-refundable, representing mutual commitment


3.4.2. Team Allocation
The number of teams assigned to each initiative will be agreed in the Project Schedule based on:
      Scope and complexity of the initiative
      Expected duration
      Target value and delivery timeframes
      Resource requirements identified during scoping
Multiple initiatives may run concurrently, each with its own team allocation and Project Schedule.

3.4.3. Minimum Deliverables Within Retainer
Each monthly retainer includes:
      Monthly performance dashboard and benefit tracking report
      Access to CATALYST 7™ tools and templates for engaged projects
      Monthly stakeholder briefing and progress review
      Continuous monitoring of asset performance against established baselines

3.5.           Value Share Mechanism
Value share is calculated and paid on an initiative-by-initiative basis. For each value-generating opportunity,
CBSE JV and TfNSW will execute a Project Schedule that documents the specific commercial terms
applicable to that initiative.
The value share payable for each initiative is calculated as follows:
Value Share = (Validated Benefits - Cumulative Retainer Paid) × Agreed Value Share Percentage
The value share is payable only after the measurement outcomes are confirmed through the validation
process documented in the Project Schedule.

3.6.           Commercial Framework
The commercial structure operationalises these principles through an integrated pricing arrangement:

    Element                        Value-Based Model
    Initial investment             40-50% of market rates
    Monthly retainer               $10,000 (non-refundable)
    Validation services            Included (via ST Engineering if applicable)
    Benefit share                  Agreed share of validated benefits above retainer
    Payment frequency              Quarterly reconciliation based on validated achievements

This structure creates a partnership dynamic fundamentally different from traditional consulting. CBSE JV
has a direct financial interest in ensuring that initiatives succeed—not merely that deliverables are
completed.
3.7.             Validation Framework
3.7.1.           Validation Principles
The validation process is governed by the following principles to ensure both rigour and efficiency:
     Transparency: All benefit calculations and supporting evidence are shared with TfNSW
     Conservatism: Where uncertainty exists, benefit estimates err on the conservative side
     Attribution: Only benefits demonstrably linked to CBSE JV interventions are included
     Timing: Benefits are recognised when realised, not when projected
     Independence: ST Engineering provides objective assessment at all tiers

3.7.2.           Tiered Validation Approach
A robust validation framework is essential to the integrity of the value-based model. Both parties must have
confidence that claimed benefits are real, measurable and attributable to the engagement. We propose a
tiered validation approach that applies appropriate scrutiny proportionate to benefit magnitude.
The following table sets out the validation requirements at each tier (may be amended in the project schedule
by agreement):

    Value Range      Validation Method                       Review Period      ST Engineering Role   Cost Sharing
    < $100,000       Self-certification with pre agreed      3 business days    Review and endorse    CBSE JV funds
                     documentation evidence
    $100,000 –       Self-certification with pre agreed      5 business days    Review and endorse    CBSE JV funds
    $250,000         documentation evidence and peer
                     review
    $250,000 –       External review accepted by nominated   10 business days   Validate and          CBSE JV funds
    $500,000         TfNSW Representative                                       benchmark
    > $500,000       As agreed in the Project Schedule       20 business days   As agreed in the      As agreeD in the
                                                                                Project Schedule      Project Schedule

3.7.3.           Benchmarking and Global Comparison
ST Engineering's involvement enables benefits to be contextualised against international standards. This
benchmarking serves two purposes: it validates that claimed improvements are genuine by reference to what
has been achieved elsewhere, and it identifies further opportunities by highlighting gaps between current
performance and global best practice.
4.          Initial (Seed) Initiatives for Consideration
4.1.        Seed List of Value-Based Initiatives
CBSE JV has developed an initial seed list of initiatives for consideration by TfNSW under the value-based
engagement model. Each initiative has been assessed for value potential, implementation complexity and
alignment with TfNSW strategic priorities. The value, duration and value share for each initiative would be
agreed prior to commencement in accordance with the model principles.
The following table presents the proposed initiatives:

                          industry context and expertise to:
                          -Validate project needs and remove un-necessary elements
                          -Validate the specifications to identify opportunities to depart

4.2.        Initiative Selection and Prioritisation
The initiatives listed above represent opportunities identified through CBSE JV's existing engagement with
TfNSW and analysis of strategic priorities. Not all initiatives need proceed simultaneously—the value-based
model allows for selective engagement based on TfNSW priorities and resource availability.
We propose the following approach to initiative selection:
1. Joint prioritisation workshop: CBSE JV and TfNSW jointly review the seed list and agree priority
   initiatives
2. Value assessment: For each priority initiative, both parties review the value hypothesis and agree target
   outcomes
3. Commercial agreement: Value target, duration, and value share percentage are agreed for each
   initiative
4. Phased commencement: Initiatives commence in agreed sequence, allowing learnings to inform
   subsequent engagements
5.        Traditional Engagement Under Collaborative Terms
To enable engagement of our core and supplementary teams at a reduced set of rates, the proposed
amendments to the contract detailed at Appendix C Table 1 must be accepted.
To enable the value-based engagement model amendments to the contract detailed at Appendix C Table 2
must be accepted.
These amendments are designed to be applied to TfNSW's Professional Services Contract (Version 4.0,
September 2024) as provided in Annexure A - Proposed Contract.
6.        Next Steps
6.1.      Engagement Process
We propose the following progression to establish the value-based partnership:

 Phase              Timing     Activities
 Framework          Weeks      Executive briefing on value-based model; ST Engineering validation
 Agreement          1-2        methodology review; selection of preferred commercial structure; initial
                               product prioritisation
 Commercial         Weeks      Contract amendments completion; governance structure establishment;
 Finalisation       3-4        validation criteria agreement; baseline methodology confirmation
 Partnership        Weeks      Team mobilisation including ST Engineering briefing; stakeholder
 Launch             5-6        communication; initial benchmarking study; commencement of first
                               initiative

6.2.      Our Commitment
 CBSE JV commits to fostering a genuine partnership with shared success, delivering validated and
measurable value, maintaining single-point accountability throughout the engagement, providing global
insights with local delivery, and creating alignment of interests that benefits both parties.
This partnership proposal is valid for 90 days and represents CBSE JV's commitment to deliver world-class,
validated asset management solutions through a commercial model that ensures our success depends on
yours.

                                                               $750,000                            $750,000

B.3. Conclusion
The value-based model protects TfNSW in downside scenarios while sharing upside with CBSE JV when
initiatives succeed. This alignment of interests creates genuine partnership focused on outcomes rather than
activity.

APPENDIX E

Catalyst7TM Measurement
Framework
Appendix E: Catalyst7TM Measurement Framework
CBSE JV Principles for Using CATALYST 7™ and Applying Value-Based Pricing

Overview
CATALYST 7™ is CBS Group's proprietary framework for delivering and measuring value across infrastructure
advisory engagements. Developed through over a decade of application and more than $1 billion in
documented savings for Transport for NSW and other major infrastructure clients, CATALYST 7™ provides a
comprehensive methodology that moves beyond traditional time-and-materials consulting to create
strategic partnerships where compensation is directly tied to measurable value creation.
The framework recognises that infrastructure value extends far beyond financial returns to encompass seven
interconnected capital dimensions, enabling CBS Group to identify, quantify, and deliver holistic value that
benefits all stakeholders.

The Seven Capitals Framework
CATALYST 7™ measures and optimises value across seven interconnected capital dimensions:

 Capital                  Description
 Financial                Direct financial returns, economic impact, cost savings, revenue generation, and asset
                          value appreciation
 Manufactured             Physical assets, infrastructure systems, asset condition and performance, and lifecycle
                          optimisation
 Intellectual             Innovations, methodologies, proprietary IP, process improvements, and
                          organisational learning
 Human                    Workforce knowledge and skills, safety culture, organisational capabilities, and talent
                          development
 Social                   Relationships and networks, community benefit, stakeholder satisfaction, social
                          licence to operate, and accessibility
 Natural                  Environmental resources, sustainability performance, carbon emissions, biodiversity,
                          and circular economy principles
 Digital                  Data assets and insights, information systems, digital transformation capabilities, and
                          AI/advanced analytics

Value Multiplication Principles
CATALYST 7™ recognises that the seven capitals form an interconnected system where interventions in one
dimension create ripple effects across others. CBS Group actively designs interventions to maximise
synergistic effects through three mechanisms:
    1. Cross-Capital Synergies: Interventions in one capital dimension enhance value creation in another
       (e.g., Digital Capital investments enhance Manufactured Capital performance while improving
       Natural Capital outcomes).
    2. Cascading Value Effects: Value created in one dimension triggers sequential value creation in others
       through mapped pathways.
    3. Reinforcing Feedback Loops: Value creation across multiple dimensions establishes self-
       reinforcing cycles that compound over time.
Typical value multipliers range from 1.8x–2.7x for two-dimensional interactions, 2.5x–3.8x for three-
dimensional synergies, and 3.2x–4.7x for comprehensive multi-dimensional integration.
Value-Based Pricing Commercial Model
7.1.1.     Dual-Component Structure
CBS Group's VBP model comprises two components designed to balance budget predictability with strong
performance incentives:
   •     Strategic Partnership Retainer: Fixed monthly fee covering strategic advisory, methodology
         development, knowledge transfer, governance support, and continuous improvement activities.
   •     Value-Based Success Fees: Calculated as a percentage of independently validated value creation
         across the seven capitals, typically representing 60–80% of total CBS compensation in successful
         engagements.

7.1.2.     Measurement and Validation
CATALYST 7™ applies rigorous measurement protocols to ensure credible value creation claims:
   •     Baseline Rigor: Comprehensive baseline assessment incorporating 3–5 years of historical
         performance data, industry benchmarks, and explicit counterfactual analysis.
   •     Conservative Estimation: Lower-bound estimates, time-value discounting, and exclusion of weakly
         attributed value categories.
   •     Independent Validation: All material value claims (>$1M) undergo third-party validation by qualified
         assessors, with full methodology and data transparency.

7.1.3.     Performance Guarantees
CBS Group typically guarantees minimum value creation of 3x–5x total retainer fees over the engagement
period, demonstrating confidence in the CATALYST 7™ methodology. Remediation mechanisms include
success fee waivers, partial retainer refunds, and extended engagement commitments if guaranteed
minimums are not achieved.

7.2.       Perfect Incentive Alignment
CATALYST 7™ VBP fundamentally restructures the consultant-client relationship:
   1. Outcome-Based Compensation: CBS Group's compensation is calculated as a percentage of
      documented value creation, ensuring CBS earns more when clients achieve more value.
   2. Risk-Sharing: CBS Group bears significant performance risk—if interventions fail to create value,
      CBS receives only the base retainer without success fees.
   3. Long-Term Partnership: Engagements typically span 3–5 years with phased success fee payments
      tied to sustained value creation.
   4. Capability Transfer: Structured knowledge transfer builds client capabilities over time, enabling
      increasingly independent value creation.

7.3.       Summary
CATALYST 7™ represents a fundamental shift from traditional consulting to genuine strategic partnership. By
measuring and optimising value across seven interconnected capitals, applying rigorous baseline and
validation protocols, and structuring commercial arrangements around shared risk and reward, CBS Group
delivers transformative outcomes where consultant success depends entirely on delivering measurable,
sustainable client value.

## Executive Summary FINAL.docx

*File: `Working finals/Executive Summary FINAL.docx`*

A Partnership of Certainty, Value, and Commitment

On behalf of the entire CBS Group, I am delighted to present our proposal to continue our valued partnership with Transport for NSW (TfNSW). 

Over the past 18 months, we have been privileged to work alongside your Asset Management team on some of the state’s most significant infrastructure programs. The trust you have placed in us is a responsibility we take very seriously. 

This proposal is our commitment to repaying that trust by delivering what we believe no other partner can: the certainty of proven performance, the security of zero transition risk, and a genuine partnership dedicated to delivering value to the people of NSW.

Our proposal is built on a foundation of certainty. In a landscape often defined by complexity and unpredictability, CBS Group offers a proven, low-risk path to achieving TfNSW’s strategic asset management objectives. 

Our track record with TfNSW speaks for itself. We have delivered over $1.049 billion in validated savings across your programs through the rigorous application of our proprietary CAPITAL Framework. This is not a theoretical model but a proven engine for value creation, demonstrated on your own assets. Our consistent 97.2% on-time delivery rate and a 4.7/5.0 client satisfaction score reflect the strength of our collaborative relationship and our deep understanding of your operational and strategic needs. These are the tangible outcomes of our mutual partnership.

Continuity is a powerful strategic advantage. Our proposal eliminates the significant risks, costs, and delays associated with onboarding a new provider. We offer TfNSW the certainty of immediate, uninterrupted productivity from Day One by proposing a core team of seven senior specialists, most of whom who are already delivering for you. This team, with over 175 years of combined experience, possesses an invaluable understanding of your assets, governance, and priorities. By choosing CBS Group, you avoid a typical 7-12 month learning curve and the associated productivity dip.

As a 100% Australian-owned SME, our success is intrinsically linked to the prosperity of NSW. We offer an agile, responsive service model that provides direct access to senior leadership, ensuring greater efficiency and value. Our commitment extends beyond the project to creating lasting social and economic benefits for the people of NSW, demonstrated through our First Nations Engineering Internship & Innovation Fund and our dedication to 95%+ local content.

In summary, CBS Group offers TfNSW a partnership built on a foundation of trust and proven results. We provide the certainty of an expert team that is already performing, a methodology that has already delivered exceptional value, and a shared commitment to the future success of Transport for NSW. We look forward to the opportunity to continue this successful journey with you.

CBS Group's unique market position ensures our recommendations serve only the State's best interests, free from the conflicts inherent in traditional advisory options.

In procuring strategic asset management advice, TfNSW must navigate three distinct risks:

Analytical capability without operational insight — rigorous analysis undermined by operations-phase blind spots, defaults to "lazy precedent," and innovation constrained by professional indemnity exposure.

Operational experience compromised by commercial interest — delivery knowledge shaped to suit the adviser's own capabilities or downstream ambitions, creating conflicts that undermine procurement integrity.

Commercial expertise without technical depth — financial and transactional structuring that lacks deep asset management knowledge, relying on fragmented specialist inputs that dilute accountability and create coordination overhead.

CBS threads this needle. Our team, structure, and independence allow us to combine top-tier analytical calibre with genuine operational experience—free from these competing tensions. With no downstream delivery contracts or transaction fees influencing our advice, we challenge established norms and drive innovation aligned solely with best value for the State.

With CBS, TfNSW has absolute certainty: impartial advice, rigorous analysis, and a singular focus on your success.

Sarah Taylor
Partner Strategy and Technology
CBS Group

December 1, 2025

## TfNSW AMSS RFP_RS2-RS8_RS10-RS11_CBS Group.docx

*File: `Working finals/TfNSW AMSS RFP_RS2-RS8_RS10-RS11_CBS Group.docx`*

Confirmation of Conditions of Participation

We are deeply grateful for the trust Transport for NSW (TfNSW) has placed in CBS Group over the past 18 months. It has been a privilege to support your critical infrastructure programs – the Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney Harbour Tunnel, and M6 Stage 1 – and to work alongside your talented Asset Management team. 

We are pleased to confirm that CBS Group meets the Conditions of Participation outlined in Section 2.1 of the RFP. We have demonstrated experience in two or more named transactions in multiple areas of expertise listed. Our recent and direct experience on the Western Harbour Tunnel (WHT), Sydney Harbour Tunnel (SHT) and M6 Stage 1 (M6) projects provides clear and irrefutable evidence of our capability.

The table below directly maps our experience against the specific areas of expertise required by TfNSW.

CBS Group submits a fully compliant offer in response to RFP No. WS5364262133 with our compliance table on the following page

Compliance to TfNSW RFP Requirements

The compliance table below, maps each requirement from the Transport for NSW RFP documents to the corresponding section(s) in the CBS Group response document. All references are by section and heading, not by page number.

Respondent’s details and declaration

Contract, insurances, financial 

Resource capability and availability

CBS Group offers TfNSW the team you already know, trust and rely on. 

Our proposed Core Team of seven senior specialists – Jeff Dusting, Jim Ellwood, Nick May, Lucas Rathmanner, George Khoury, David Harper and Kate Heath – are generally not new faces requiring introduction; they are the proven professionals currently delivering exceptional results on your Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney Harbour Tunnel and M6 Stage 1 projects. 

By selecting CBS Group, TfNSW eliminates the single greatest risk in any professional services engagement: the uncertainty of working with an unfamiliar team using untested methodologies. Instead, you gain immediate operational readiness and continue a partnership that is already delivering measurable value.  

Our team's experience averages 25+ years per person, totalling 175+ years of combined expertise in asset management, tolling, ITS, operations and lifecycle planning. More importantly, we know your assets, your governance frameworks, your stakeholders and your strategic priorities. This is a proposal for future performance and a commitment to continue the excellent performance you are benefitting from today.

Resource capability and availability

Response synopsis:

Our Core Team is the team you know – led by Jeff and Jim. Nick, George, Lucas and David will continue the specialist expertise that have delivered success over the last two years. With no learning curve, they will add value from Day 1. The addition of Kate Heath brings timely and relevant market and transaction knowledge to the team, as she leverages expertise gained in senior roles at Downer, Ventia, Broadspectrum and Cushman Wakefield.

To provide TfNSW with ultimate flexibility, this Core Team is supplemented by named specialists and a strategic partnership with Resolve Group. TfNSW can access these resources for temporary expertise or surge capacity to meet expedited timelines.

CBS Group has completed Annexure B with our resource capability and availability, which is located with our Section 9 Pricing response. Below we provide more detail on the capability and coverage of our team against TfNSW’s service requirements and outcomes for the AMSS contract.

Core Team: Seven specialists with 175+ years’ combined experience

Our lean 7.0 FTE structure comprehensively covers all RFP requirements with deep specialisation:

Jeff Dusting (Project Director) – ISO 55001 CFAM, CAPITAL Framework author, 25+ years’ experience

Jim Ellwood (Principal Asset Advisor) – ISO 55001 credentials, 25+ years’ asset management experience

Nick May (Tolling & Systems Lead) – Video-only tolling pioneer, 25+ years’ ITS expertise

George Khoury, David Harper, Lucas Rathmanner (Specialist Technical Advisors) – O&M, financial modelling, systems engineering 

Kate Heath (Principal Commercial and Transaction Advisor) – contract and project management, commercial negotiations, data and document management

This team delivers comprehensive coverage of all six RFP service areas, all seven Initial Period deliverables and all 10 required skill sets.

Resource availability summary

Immediate productivity and seamless transition

Most Core Team members are currently working on TfNSW programs. We know TfNSW assets, governance frameworks, stakeholders, systems and strategic priorities. This enables 100% Day 1 productivity.

Knowledge transfer and capability building

CBS Group is committed to continuing our collaborative deliver model with TfNSW, which grows capability in TfNSW and embeds knowledge transfer between and within both our organisations. TfNSW staff work alongside CBS consultants, building lasting capability that reduces long-term reliance on external advisors. More detail on our approach is included in Section 4.3.

A trusted partner for TfNSW across the duration of the contract

To further assure you that you can rely on our capability and track record, we commit to maintain our nominated Core Team through all delivery phases (Initial Period, Option Periods, Transition-Out). This commitment is established in contractual protections. Resource allocation remains flexible to TfNSW’s needs:

Supplementary Resources 

We offer TfNSW the ability to engage additional support more rapidly, and in a commercially simpler way, by drawing on supplementary team resources not included in our fixed price. These additional resources are available to provide TfNSW with a more extensive breadth of subject matter expertise and capability which we have mapped to additional activities that may be required over the course of the contract term. Key supplementary resources are included in the organisation chart in Section 4.2.1. We have included the Supplementary Team with our resource activity mapping, their bio’s and full CV’s in Attachment C. 

Team structure and subcontractor relationships

Response synopsis:

CBS Group's offer is for our dedicated Core Team, organised into a three-tiered hierarchy to deliver all core RFP requirements with proven expertise and 100% Day One productivity. 

Our key resources are specialists, and each is cross skilled across many AMSS required skill sets to provide technical, operational and commercial advisory. This brings a high level of quality and productivity, and reduces inefficiencies that often arise in handovers across disciplines. 

Our nominated team provides all required skills and services to rapidly realise TfNSW outcomes with no proposed sub contractors.

We propose to maintain this structure and leadership team through all delivery phases, and may add team leads at level two should supplementary support be engaged to support additional tasks. 

Organisation and delivery hierarchy: Certainty with a cohesive, proven team

CBS Group proposes a collaborative, integrated Core Team structure to deliver seamless service across all RFP requirements. The three tiers of our structure provide clear accountability and governance; enable autonomous, expert-led execution; and make deep specialist expertise available across all service areas.

The team is a lean, highly experienced group of senior experts. TfNSW gains direct access to the highest levels of strategic advice and technical expertise. The team is led by Jeff Dusting as the primary point of contact and engagement lead. He will assure the overall quality and ongoing value in the delivery of the Services.

Organisational design

Our structure is flat and collaborative, reflecting our ‘one team’ approach. This ensures that TfNSW has a clear and direct line of communication to the specific subject matter expert best placed to answer their query, while maintaining a single point of accountability for the overall engagement.

No team member leads any other internal or external team. All team leadership activity undertaken by any member of the Core Team is directed solely to the TfNSW teams and activities.

Roles and responsibilities

Our people: Your proven team

CBS Group proposes the exact team of senior experts who established and delivered the Western Harbour Tunnel, Sydney Harbour Tunnel and M6 Stage 1 asset management contracts. These are the professionals who've been integral to your program's success from inception.

Our team includes:

The principal architects of the AM Contract Model (CAPITAL Framework) as applied to WHT, SHT and M6

Key personnel who led your tender evaluations and developed operational concepts

Technical specialists who engineered systems for these specific assets

Tier 1: Strategic leadership and accountability

Jeff Dusting – Strategic oversight and accountability

Jeff serves as the single point of accountability to TfNSW, providing strategic oversight, executive relationship management and ultimate quality assurance across all deliverables.

Unique value: As the author of the CAPITAL Framework that has delivered $1.049B1 in validated savings for TfNSW, Jeff brings exceptional strategic asset management expertise.

Tier 2: Service stream leadership

Two empowered delivery leads manage day-to-day execution in distinct service streams, reporting directly to Jeff Dusting. These are senior experts who make operational decisions autonomously within agreed frameworks, ensuring responsive and agile service delivery.

Jim Ellwood – Technical Services Lead

Jim leads Technical Services, encompassing asset management planning, lifecycle cost modelling, due diligence and technical assurance.

Unique value: Jim's deep experience in the application of ISO 55001 asset management principles ensures rigorous, defensible technical analysis aligned with international standards.

Nick May – Tolling & Systems Specialist Lead

Nick leads the specialist technical resources for tolling systems and technology, working closely with Jim Ellwood and others to optimise delivery of services.

Unique value: Nick is the only expert in video-only tolling in the Australian market, providing TfNSW with access to specialised expertise not available elsewhere.

Tier 3: Specialist advisors

Four specialist advisors provide domain expertise across technical stream including tolling, operations, WOL analysis and ITS systems, as well as contracting and commercial management. These specialists are assigned to specific workstreams as needed, receiving technical direction from the service stream leads, while remaining accountable to Jeff Dusting as the Project Director. This is in keeping with our non-hierarchical organisational approach that supports collaboration, integration and innovation while maintaining accountability and productivity.

Lucas Rathmanner – ITS & Systems Engineering Specialist

Unique value: Lucas's expertise in ITS, tolling and control systems across many industries delivers WOL value across complex transport infrastructure. His proven track record includes developing technical and systems engineering specifications for the unique WHT tolling solution, KPI and performance modelling and leading the development of a pragmatic solution and specification for the SHT OMCS.

George Khoury – Operations & Maintenance Specialist

Unique value: George's practical, hands-on operational experience ensures that asset management strategies are operationally viable and deliverable.

David Harper – WOL Analysis Specialist

Unique value: David's expertise in ITS asset lifecycle planning and TotEx modelling delivers WOL value across complex transport infrastructure. His proven track record includes developing national ITS lifecycle strategies and development of pre tender estimates, commercial performance modelling in support of a number of TfNSW AM initiatives over the past 3 years demonstrates exceptional financial modelling capability and precision.

Kate Heath – Contract Management & Commercial Specialist

Unique value: Kate’s industry experience and expertise in contract and project management together with commercial negotiations and collaborative stakeholder engagement has been developed across a diverse range of industries.

Comprehensive coverage of required skill sets

The table below demonstrates comprehensive coverage of all 10 mandatory skill sets specified in RFP Section 1.6. Each capability area has designated primary and backup resources, ensuring continuity and depth across all service requirements.

Management and governance

Clear accountability and efficient delivery

Single point of accountability

Weekly status updates to your Project Manager

Risk oversight and escalation management

Quality assurance and governance

Streamlined service delivery

Additional Technical Advisors join specific workstreams as needed, operating through an efficient matrix structure that maintains clear reporting lines without creating bottlenecks.

Why this structure delivers results

Strategic oversight without bottlenecks Jeff's accountability doesn't slow delivery because his involvement focuses on strategic decisions and quality gates, not daily tasks. Service leads have full authority to manage their streams, escalating only when TfNSW engagement is required.

Proven through current success This exact structure operates successfully on our WHT and M6 engagements, where Jeff maintains overall accountability while the team delivers autonomously with exceptional results.

Empowered team leadership Each deliverable has a designated lead who manages execution independently within agreed frameworks, makes operational decisions without constant oversight and reports through established governance channels. This balance of accountability and autonomy ensures both consistency and agility in our service delivery.

Expected relationships with subcontractors

Key principles of our subcontractor engagement approach:

Subcontractors are not required for core service delivery

Subcontractors provide additional depth and surge capacity when beneficial to TfNSW

All subcontractor work is managed and quality-assured by the CBS Core Team

Subcontractor engagement is transparent and approved by TfNSW in advance

Resource stability extends to subcontractors: We commit to the consistent availability of named individuals; there will be no post-award substitutions without TfNSW approval.

CBS Group's Core Team delivers the RFP requirements without reliance on subcontractors. To bring TfNSW additional depth, flexibility and specialisations, we’ve partnered with Resolve, a leading infrastructure advisory.

Resolve Group (New Zealand)

Relationship: Strategic partner for specialised asset management expertise

Capability: ISO 55001 certified asset management consultancy with deep rail and road expertise

Application: Deep-dive asset management planning, international benchmarking, specialised technical reviews

Engagement Model: Can be engaged under this agreement or alternate offers as beneficial to TfNSW

Governance: All Resolve Group work managed and quality-assured by CBS (Jeff Dusting, Jim Ellwood)

Please refer to Section 11 Other Parties for more information on Resolve Group.

Knowledge and experience transfer

Response synopsis:

Our Code of Conduct – Do Good, For Good, with Good People – shapes how we approach our work, our relationships and our impact on the world. We foster a culture of peer review and constructive feedback, working transparently and collaboratively as a unified team with our clients and their employees.

Our knowledge sharing takes two key pathways. The first is internal dissemination of our organisational knowledge, systems, expertise and experience within our own teams. This ensures we perform at a high standard and are job ready from Day 1. See Section 4.3.1 for detail.

The second is external transfer of knowledge to our clients’ teams, which creates systemic value by building ongoing capability, and delivers ongoing mentoring and guidance throughout our tenure. Rather than transferring knowledge as a separate activity at the end of a contract, we embed knowledge transfer into every interaction through collaborative delivery, transparent methodologies and explanatory documentation in addition to providing formal training and capability building. Our goal is to leave TfNSW better equipped to manage asset management challenges independently. See Section 4.3.2 for detail.

Internal knowledge dissemination within CBS Group

Working with TfNSW's talented teams has enhanced our capabilities, and CBS Group has established systems to capture, codify and transfer knowledge and experience from projects across our team.

All Core Team members actively contribute and have access to the CBS Knowledge Library. For example, Nick May's video-only tolling expertise developed on WHT is now codified and available for application on future projects.

For this engagement, structured onboarding is not required as all Core Team members are currently engaged on TfNSW programs. However, if Discretionary Resources are engaged, they will receive structured onboarding to ensure they are immediately effective.

The Core Team's participation in knowledge sharing activities helps ensure insights from TfNSW engagements are shared across CBS, and that best practices from other projects are brought to TfNSW.

External knowledge transfer to TfNSW

Case Study: Following the WHT tender evaluation that delivered $187M in savings, CBS’ post-project review identified:

- Success factor: Systematic application of the CAPITAL Framework's risk transfer optimisation methodology

-Improvement opportunity: Earlier engagement with the Asset Manager market to refine performance incentive structures

Action: Insights were immediately applied to SHT and M6, creating $1.049B1 in total validated savings.

CBS Group will build TfNSW's internal capability with embedded knowledge transfer. This is integral to our delivery approach.

Integrated learning through daily collaboration

We embed knowledge transfer in all aspects of delivery. Your team gains practical expertise with hands-on experience:

Side-by-side delivery – TfNSW staff work directly with CBS consultants, learning methodologies through active participation

Open-book approach – Full transparency of our CAPITAL Framework, models and tools

Detailed documentation – Every deliverable includes methodology explanations, enabling independent replication

Active mentoring – Ongoing guidance builds confidence and capability

Structured capability building

In addition to daily knowledge sharing, we provide formal development opportunities:

Proven track record:

40+ hours of training delivered to TfNSW staff

Six team members mentored in advanced methodologies

Core topics: CAPITAL Framework, lifecycle modelling, risk optimisation, sinking fund analysis

Ongoing program:

Quarterly 4-hour workshops on specialised topics and on-demand sessions addressing identified gaps

Professional certification support (ISO 55001 and others)

Lasting resources

Comprehensive methodology documentation

TfNSW-specific case study library (WHT, SHT, M6)

Captured lessons and improvement insights

Measurable outcomes

Enhanced capabilities

Independent application of the CAPITAL Framework

Internal development of lifecycle models and forecasts

Sophisticated evaluation of Asset Manager proposals

Increased self-sufficiency

Routine asset management performed internally

External support needed only for specialised requirements

Long-term cost reductions through reduced advisor dependency

This builds your internal expertise, strengthening TfNSW beyond the contract term and delivering enduring value.

Responsiveness and client feedback

CBS Group's approach to this engagement is built on genuine partnership, which requires not just delivering quality work, but also being responsive to TfNSW's needs and continuously improving based on your feedback.

Responsiveness mechanisms

We commit to the following responsiveness standards to ensure TfNSW receives timely support:

Client feedback loops

CBS Group actively seeks and responds to TfNSW feedback to continuously improve our service and remain aligned with your evolving needs.

Track record on current TfNSW engagements:

Our responsiveness and feedback approach is not theoretical – it's how we currently work with TfNSW:

Our Commitment to TfNSW:

CBS Group commits to maintaining and enhancing our responsiveness to feedback and our integrated response to feedback throughout this contract:

✅ We will respond to your requests within 24 hours (4 hours for urgent matters)

✅ We will actively seek your feedback through quarterly surveys, monthly meetings and deliverable-specific requests

✅ We will act on your feedback, acknowledging within 24 hours and acting/implementing in 1–2 weeks

✅ We will be transparent about our performance, sharing satisfaction ratings and improvement actions

✅ We will continuously improve based on your feedback, lessons learned and emerging best practices

Our philosophy is simple: Your success is our success. We measure our performance not only by our own high standards, but also – and primarily – by your satisfaction with our work.

Team quality and retention

Response synopsis:

CBS Group maintains a high-quality, experienced team through a proven three-pillar approach: 

1. Rigorous recruitment that targets only senior professionals with 15+ years of experience and demonstrated track records

2. Continuous professional development that keeps our team at the forefront of industry best practice through formal training and industry engagement 

3. Comprehensive talent management strategies that ensure stability and continuity. This approach has delivered 100% retention of our Core Team over 18 months on current TfNSW engagements.

Rigorous recruitment and selection

CBS recruits only senior, experienced professionals with proven track records. Recruitment criteria include:

Continuous professional development

CBS Group invests significantly in the continuous professional development for all team members to ensure they remain at the forefront of industry best practice.

Example: Jeff Dusting regularly presents on the CAPITAL Framework at Asset Management Council conferences, and Nick May has presented on video-only tolling at ITS Australia events.

Retention strategies

CBS Group recognises that maintaining a high quality and experienced team requires not just recruitment and development, but also retention. Our retention strategies ensure stability and continuity for TfNSW.

Track record of retention

CBS Group's retention strategies are proven effective:

100% retention on current TfNSW engagements:

Zero voluntary turnover of Core Team members over 18 months on WHT, SHT, M6

Zero involuntary turnover (no performance-related terminations)

Same team delivering exceptional results continuously

Firm-wide retention well above industry average:

Annual voluntary turnover rate: <10% (industry average: 20–40%)

Average tenure of senior consultants: 8+ years

Multiple team members with 10+ years at CBS Group

Police checks

Response synopsis:

CBS Group confirms that formal Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Checks (NCCHCs) will be completed for all proposed key personnel in accordance with TfNSW requirements, using WorkPro's Digital Identity Solution for accuracy, fraud prevention and full compliance with Australian Government standards. 

All proposed team members currently hold valid police checks as part of their existing TfNSW engagement on the WHT, SHT and M6 projects. These will be refreshed as required to ensure compliance throughout the new contract term. 

Beyond police checks, all CBS Group personnel undergo comprehensive background verification, including employment history, qualification verification, professional membership verification and reference checks from previous clients.

Copies of NCCHC certificates will be provided to TfNSW upon request.

Collective skills and experience

Response synopsis:

CBS Group has provided comprehensive CVs for our nominated Core Team in Attachment B using the template provided in the RFP.  

Our Core Team of seven specialists collectively represents 175+ years of combined experience, averaging 25+ years per team member, with proven expertise spanning roads, rail, tolling, ITS, operations and asset management.  This team has collaboratively delivered $1.049B1 in validated savings for TfNSW through the systematic application of the CAPITAL Framework.

We have also provided CVs for our Supplementary Team members in Attachment C.  

Client Testimonial

"CBS Group's strategic asset management advice has been instrumental in achieving value optimisation across our tunnel portfolio. Their CAPITAL Framework methodology delivered outcomes that exceeded our expectations, and their team's deep understanding of our governance requirements ensures seamless collaboration."

– TfNSW Senior Executive, WHT

Demonstrated experience and ability to perform the services

CBS Group brings TfNSW a powerful combination: proven capability with immediate readiness. 

As your current provider on the WHT, SHT and M6 projects, we offer the continuation of the exceptional performance you are already experiencing. 

The seven-person team led by Jeff Dusting, Jim Ellwood, and Nick May will deliver using the proven CAPITAL Framework methodology that has already generated considerable savings for TfNSW and notable performance metrics across our existing contracts. 

For TfNSW, this means zero transition risk, immediate mobilisation within three days (seven days faster than your requirement) and avoidance of transition costs.

Our track record is independently validated by your own project records, verifiable by your own team members and grounded in the trustworthy partnership we have built together. 

Our responses to Returnable Schedules 5 and 6 are intended to show our capability and alignment to the RFP Scope, demonstrating that we will deliver the required services to levels that meet or exceed the anticipated outcomes.

How CBS will deliver the services

Response synopsis:

CBS Group will deliver the Asset Management Support Services through the same experienced team you already know and trust: Jeff Dusting (Project Director), Jim Ellwood (Technical Services Lead), Nick May (Tolling & Systems Services Lead), along with Lucas Rathmanner, George Khoury, David Harper, and new member, Kate Heath. All are specialists, and all except new member Kate are currently delivering exceptional results on your WHT, SHT and M6 projects using CAPITAL methodologies that deliver substantial, verified savings to TfNSW. 

The team will apply our integrated delivery model built on three foundational pillars: 

1. Immediate mobilisation

2. Systematic value delivery through our proven CAPITAL Framework methodology 

3. Partnership collaboration that builds TfNSW's internal capability through knowledge transfer, transparent methods and collaborative decision-making. 

This approach eliminates transition risk, avoids transition costs and ensures the exceptional performance you are currently experiencing continues uninterrupted through the contract term.

Detail on our delivery of required services and outcomes is in Section 5.1.2 below, with detail on our roles and required skill sets included in Section 4.2.2. 

Immediate mobilisation and seamless transition

CBS Group is currently delivering asset management advisory services to TfNSW. Our existing engagements enable us to offer unparalleled continuity and immediate operational readiness.

Our proven partnership delivers immediate value

Our team has already built the critical foundations for success: established workflows, proven systems integration and trusted stakeholder relationships across relevant TfNSW divisions. 

This brings immediate, tangible advantages. Our consultants hold current security clearances, possess deep familiarity with TfNSW's governance frameworks and have successfully integrated our jointly-developed CAPITAL platform with your data systems. Through years of successful collaboration, we've refined our stakeholder engagement protocols to align seamlessly with your operations.

The strategic value of the continuity we bring cannot be overstated. A new provider is likely to require many months to achieve the operational maturity we already possess, and TfNSW would likely see reduced service quality, knowledge gaps and relationship disruptions during that learning period. 

CBS Group offers the immediate, uninterrupted continuation of the exceptional service delivery you currently experience in our WHT and M6 engagements, alongside all the other benefits we bring to the contract, as outlined in this proposal.

Delivery of services and service outcomes

Delivery of the services in accordance with the RFP scope

This table shows how CBS Group's Core Team comprehensively covers all RFP service areas, with clear leadership assignments and proven experience.

R Principal Expert

P Expert

Team coverage of the two-phase service outcomes

This table shows CBS Group's comprehensive capability to deliver all service outcomes across the initial period and subsequent option periods, with clear accountability assignments (using RACI: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed) and proven relevant experience on current TfNSW projects.

Systematic value delivery through the CAPITAL Framework

CAPITAL Framework: Proven Excellence in Asset Management.

At the heart of our service delivery is the award-winning CAPITAL Framework, co-developed with TfNSW as our signature methodology for strategic asset management advisory services. With 15 years of refinement through infrastructure project delivery, CAPITAL has achieved $1.049B1 in validated savings across TfNSW's current project portfolio – delivering an impressive 31% average optimisation of contract values.

This represents proven operational capability, not theoretical promise. The CAPITAL Framework combines our unique methodology, sophisticated tools and specialised team expertise to deliver quantified, measurable value on TfNSW's most critical infrastructure assets. Having been specifically tailored to your operating environment through our collaborative development process, it stands as a distinctive competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated or quickly established by alternative providers.

The seven core capabilities 

The CAPITAL Framework integrates seven core capabilities in a systematic approach.  It is comprehensively aligned with ISO 55000, the internationally recognised standard for asset management:

C – Comprehensive asset baseline: 

We establish accurate, detailed foundations for asset data. These cover all infrastructure components, technical specifications, performance standards and maintenance requirements.

This capability has enabled us to develop robust pre-tender estimates and WOL cost models for the WHT and M6 projects.

A – Asset performance analysis: 

We apply data-driven condition assessment and failure rate analysis. We leverage actual performance data from TfNSW's existing portfolio (SHT, M5, M8) to develop evidence-based performance models.

This capability delivered $58M in savings from maintenance interval optimisation on the WHT project.

P – Portfolio integration: 

We optimise across multiple assets through cross-asset learning and benchmarking, identifying opportunities that emerge from TfNSW's broader portfolio context. 

This capability has enabled us to apply lessons learned from the WHT engagement to deliver enhanced outcomes on the M6 project.

I – Investment optimisation: 

We apply sophisticated WOL cost modelling and trade-off analysis. This identifies opportunities for value optimisation.

This capability delivered $74M in lifecycle replacement strategy savings on the WHT project. We achieved this through condition-based approaches, as opposed to fixed replacement cycles.

T – Technical excellence: 

We ensure systems engineering rigour and technical assurance through multi-disciplinary analysis and validation. This capability ensures all optimisation strategies are technically sound, operationally deliverable and compliant with safety and performance requirements.

A – Asset Manager alignment: 

We create collaborative contract structures and performance incentives. These align Asset Manager behaviour with TfNSW's objectives.

This capability delivered $55M in combined savings on the WHT project. This was achieved through risk transfer optimisation ($38M) and performance incentive design ($17M).

L – Lifecycle management: 

We ensure long-term sustainability through robust governance frameworks, continuous improvement processes and knowledge transfer.

This capability has delivered 100% accuracy in sinking fund forecasts over the past 18 months on M6.

Application across service lines

Delivering excellence across every service area

The proven CAPITAL Framework powers our comprehensive delivery across all three service areas, bringing the same exceptional results we've achieved on WHT and M6 to your entire portfolio.

Technical services: We apply CAPITAL's asset performance analysis and investment optimisation capabilities to develop robust asset management plans, WOL cost strategies, and sinking fund frameworks. This yields the same value optimisation that has generated significant savings on our current TfNSW engagements.

Transaction services: Our portfolio integration and Asset Manager alignment capabilities support commercial negotiations, manage variations and facilitate partner communications with precision. We bring to every transaction the same strategic insight that delivered $187M in WHT savings, ensuring maximum value capture throughout the contract lifecycle.

General services: CAPITAL's comprehensive asset baseline and lifecycle management capabilities enable high-quality reporting, documentation and administrative support. We maintain the exceptional performance standards demonstrated across our TfNSW portfolio – including our 97.2% on-time delivery rate and consistently high stakeholder satisfaction ratings.

Each service area benefits from our proven methodology, established systems integration, and deep institutional knowledge, ensuring consistent excellence and measurable value delivery from Day 1.

The CAPITAL Framework: The engine of our strategic capability

The engine driving our capability is the award-winning CAPITAL Framework. It provides the methodology to ensure that every service we deliver is strategically focused, commercially astute and optimised for WOL value. 

Key differentiators of the CAPITAL Framework:

ISO 55000 alignment delivering global best practice for asset management excellence

CBS Group's CAPITAL Framework aligns with or exceeds the requirements of ISO 55000, the internationally recognised standard for asset management. This ensures that our approach is not only proven through our track record with TfNSW but also validated against global best practice and standards.

ISO 55000 provides a comprehensive framework for organisations to develop, implement and maintain their asset management systems with a focus on value realisation, stakeholder alignment, risk-based decision-making and continuous improvement. By demonstrating the CAPITAL Framework's comprehensive alignment with ISO 55000, we prove to TfNSW that our methodology is world-class and grounded in internationally recognised principles. Below, we map ISO 55000 requirements and CAPITAL Framework components.

Why ISO 55000 alignment matters to TfNSW:

Internationally recognised principles: ISO 55000 represents the consensus of global asset management experts, ensuring our approach incorporates best practices from around the world.

Systematic and comprehensive: The standard provides a structured approach to managing assets throughout their lifecycle, ensuring no critical aspects are overlooked.

Value-focused: ISO 55000 emphasises value realisation for stakeholders, aligning perfectly with TfNSW's requirement for commercially astute, outcome-focused advice.

Risk-based decision-making: The standard requires systematic consideration of risks and opportunities, supporting TfNSW's need for balanced, forward-looking strategic advice.

Continuous improvement: ISO 55000's emphasis on ongoing refinement ensures our methodologies evolve and improve over time, as demonstrated by our application of WHT lessons to M6.

CAPITAL Framework alignment with ISO 55000 key requirements:

Broader CAPITAL Framework impact across TfNSW projects

The WHT case study demonstrates the CAPITAL Framework's effectiveness on a single project. However, the Framework's value is proven across our entire TfNSW portfolio. The table below summarises the cumulative validated savings achieved through systematic application of the CAPITAL Framework.

Our track record demonstrates that the CAPITAL Framework consistently delivers significant, validated cost savings across diverse asset types, contract structures and project contexts. 

The Framework's systematic, data-driven approach ensures that savings are achieved through genuine optimisation of asset management strategies, rather than reduced service quality or increased risk.
