## CBS RFP WIP Attachment C_RS 4 Supplementary Resource CVs.docx

*File: `Working finals/Conforming attachments/Att C Supplementary Resource cvs/CBS RFP WIP Attachment C_RS 4 Supplementary Resource CVs.docx`*

Executive Summary

This document presents bio pen pics for CBS Group team members, highlighting their expertise and experience relevant to TfNSW's complex asset management and transport infrastructure projects. Each profile captures the essential skills, project achievements, and unique value proposition of team members.

The CBS team brings together complementary expertise essential for TfNSW's complex asset management and transport infrastructure projects. The team combines strategic leadership and transformation experience, safety assurance and systems engineering expertise, environmental and sustainability management capabilities, digital transformation and innovation skills, and proven project delivery track records.

Key collective capabilities include:

•	Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) and operational technology

•	Safety assurance and systems engineering for critical infrastructure

•	Environmental management and sustainability certification

•	Digital transformation and data analytics

•	Asset management and maintenance optimization

•	Defence engineering and security protocols

•	Strategic innovation and commercialization

•	Organizational change and program delivery

•	Stakeholder engagement across all levels of government

This combination of expertise positions CBS Group as an ideal partner for TfNSW in delivering complex transport infrastructure projects that require sophisticated technical capabilities, rigorous safety and quality standards, and effective stakeholder management.

Team Profiles

Sarah Taylor

Strategic Technology Leader - Tolling Systems & Digital Transformation

Sarah is a seasoned innovation strategist with decades of experience developing new products and services to create multi-million-dollar market opportunities across APAC and the USA. With a remarkable track record of identifying latent intellectual property and transforming underperforming divisions into high-value acquisitions, she has delivered over $500m in revenue through strategic licensing, created $57m annual revenue streams through innovative channel development, and facilitated multiple successful exits including businesses acquired at 10x valuations within 18 months. Her expertise spans conceptualising new revenue models, establishing strategic alliances, negotiating complex commercial arrangements, and rapidly scaling market share for technology and professional services firms preparing for acquisition or listing. Sarah's unique ability to identify untapped opportunities, combined with her extensive international network of engineers, technologists, and IP specialists, makes her an exceptional strategic advisor for TfNSW's innovation and commercialisation initiatives.

Adam Noonan

Environmental, Planning & Sustainability Manager

Adam is a highly driven environmental professional with extensive experience in planning and environmental management across all project phases, currently specialising in major transport infrastructure projects. Having successfully delivered environmental impact assessments for the $6bn Western Harbour Tunnel and Beaches Link programs, he brings deep expertise in managing National Environmental Significance (NES) assessments, EPBC Act compliance, and complex stakeholder negotiations with government agencies, including DPE, NSW EPA, and Commonwealth departments. His proven track record spans environmental compliance, contaminated land remediation, including PFAS management, and Infrastructure Sustainability Council (ISC) accreditation, with strengths in post-approval documentation, consistency assessments, and construction environmental management plans. Adam's unique combination of technical environmental expertise, project management capabilities, and established relationships across government agencies positions him as an invaluable asset for managing environmental and sustainability aspects of TfNSW's complex infrastructure programs.

Gethin Perry

Strategic Asset Management & Projects Specialist

A leader with more than 25 years of experience in the development, operation and maintenance of transport projects and assets that improve customer experience and community outcomes, focussed on asset management, infrastructure, and operational technology. Proven ability to develop and lead teams and achieve change. Practical and objective with the ability to prioritise challenges and deliver business value.

Led a multidisciplinary team to perform a bottom up rebuild of the capital investment plans (0 to 25 year) and portfolio governance processes for Transurban’s Sydney motorways to ensure the safe and reliable journeys for 1 million daily customers, whilst meeting government/regulator requirements and stakeholder expectations. The prioritised, risk based plans provided the confidence for executive decision making, and enabled a 50% increase in delivery through improved project development and governance processes.

Proven ability to develop long-term strategic plans aligned to regulatory frameworks, optimise project pipelines, and deliver enterprise-level transformation. Adept at engaging Boards, regulators, and cross-functional stakeholders to enable sustainable investment decision-making and portfolio performance. A people leader dedicated to identifying, mentoring and promoting talented individuals to support their career growth and enable succession planning. 

Simon Irving

Strategic & Transformational Leader - Engineering, Delivery & Operations

Simon is a Chartered Professional Engineer with over 20 years of international experience in engineering leadership, project delivery, and asset management of large-scale critical infrastructure, specialising in Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) and operational technology. Currently serving as Senior Engineering Manager at Transurban, he has led the successful delivery of major transport infrastructure projects, including the West Gate Tunnel ITS program and CityLink Tullamarine Widening, while driving enterprise-wide transformation initiatives in engineering governance, risk management, and strategic asset planning. His expertise spans systems engineering, functional safety, and operational resilience in 24/7 mission-critical environments, with notable innovations including Australian-first implementations in machine learning traffic incident detection, tunnel air monitoring technology, and autonomous vehicle infrastructure. Simon's collaborative leadership style and focus on continuous improvement make him ideally suited for complex transport asset management programs, where he brings proven experience in stakeholder engagement, investment prioritization, and the integration of advanced technologies to enhance safety, efficiency, and user experience.

Nick Patras

ITS & Systems Engineering Specialist

Executive leader with 30+ years in the defence and transport sectors across the complete systems life cycle in the technology and engineering fields. Recognised with a systematic and strategic mindset driving transformation across organisations. Proven ability establishing leading and growing high performance teams and delivering measurable business outcomes.

Significant expertise in leading teams and project delivery across major transport clients including Transurban and TfNSW. 

Aaron Miles 

Systems Integration & Project Delivery

Aaron Miles is an experienced and effective Systems Integration / Assurance and Project Delivery lead with over 20 years of experience in the defence, rail and road industries, supported by extensive experience in commercial negotiation and management. He has successfully led delivery of complex systems integration projects from development into operations with a proven history of pragmatically tailoring systems, processes and functions to deliver the right outcome without over-complication. He prides himself on his ability to develop a cohesive workplace environment and build high-performing multi-disciplinary teams in complex commercial and engineering environments.

Reuben Welschen

Systems Engineering Assurance and Configuration Management Specialist

Reuben has more than 20 years’ experience in Systems Engineering, Assurance and Configuration Management in the Infrastructure sector (Metro, Heavy Rail, Light Rail and Road) in Australia, Asia and Europe; He has specialist skills in Systems Engineering Management, RAMS, System Safety, Risk, tunnel and fire safety across the full lifecycle from strategy and planning to design, construction, operational readiness and asset management.   

He has excellent Stakeholder, Bid and Project Management and leadership skills.  A hard working and dedicated team member and leader; visionary, commercially astute and goal driven, but also facilitating, consultative and open minded. 

Scott Graby

Senior Project Manager & Electrical Engineer

A senior project manager and chartered professional engineer with over sixteen years of experience in the delivery of complex civil infrastructure projects in both private and public sectors. Extensive experience in the design and commissioning of OMCS systems, particularly in the context of motorway management, in addition to a detailed understanding of motorway roadside tolling systems, as well as associated backoffice requirements and business systems associated with revenue recovery practices.

Voytek Szczepanik

Senior Project Management Specialist

Accomplished senior project manager with over 25 years of distinguished experience delivering complex road control systems and ITS infrastructure projects across NSW's critical transport assets. Proven expertise in SCATS implementation, tolling systems, OMCS upgrades, and integrated traffic management solutions.  

Demonstrated success managing multi-million dollar TfNSW projects including the SCATS Congestion Improvement Program and Tolling System Upgrade Program (TSUP). Expert in coordinating multi-stakeholder environments, managing technology integration, and ensuring seamless operational transitions for 24/7 critical infrastructure. 

Hossein Paras

ITS & Systems Engineer 

An accomplished Systems Architect and Program Manager with over 20 years of experience designing and delivering innovative solutions for intelligent transport systems, SCADA platforms, and business intelligence applications and worked on major infrastructure projects such as WestConnex and WGT. With expertise spans system architecture, integration, and project lifecycle management, having successfully led projects such as traffic control systems, Incident response systems, live situational awareness tools, and real-time data pipelines. I excel in collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, engaging stakeholders, and aligning technical solutions with strategic objectives. Delivered scalable, future-ready architectures for OMCS, IMS, AVs, and smart mobility solutions.   

Glen Archibald

Project Manager and Strategic Asset Management Specialist

Infrastructure project and asset manager with 25+ years’ experience in delivering major infrastructure projects and large-scale property developments. Recognised for driving efficiency, profitability and risk management through strategic planning, stakeholder collaboration and strong leadership of multi-disciplinary teams. Known for a strong commercial focus, ability to optimise operational performance, and deliver complex projects on time and within budget.

Successfully led a $200M upgrade of the Westlink M7 tolling system within the planned schedule and budget whilst maintaining service quality and mitigating risks, a $25M Westlink M7 resheeting program within the planned schedule and budget whilst maintaining the operational needs of the motorway, and secured board approval for major upgrades to the M7 through robust financial feasibility studies, directly contributing to asset value growth.

Akshitha Gorla

Data Analytics & Machine Learning Specialist

Akshitha is a motivated data analytics professional currently pursuing her Master of Science in Data Analytics at the University of Technology Sydney, bringing expertise in Python, SQL, machine learning, and data visualisation. With hands-on experience in developing ML models, including IoT intrusion detection systems achieving 87% accuracy, facial recognition systems with 98.5% accuracy, and database management systems, she demonstrates strong technical capabilities in solving complex data challenges. Her portfolio showcases proficiency in scikit-learn, TensorFlow, Tableau, and database normalisation, with practical applications spanning network security, computer vision, and business intelligence dashboard development. Akshitha's combination of technical skills in AI/ML development, data engineering capabilities, and fresh perspective on emerging analytics technologies positions her as a valuable contributor to TfNSW's data-driven decision-making and digital transformation initiatives.

Alessandro Uberti

RAMS & Safety Specialist

Alessandro is one of the most reputable RAMS specialists in Australia and is a technical lead for RAMS. He has a passional for Model Based RAMS analysis and more than 20 years’ experience in systems engineering and reliability, maintainability and safety management for high integrity and complex systems in rail, road and aviation. With a systematic, organised and agile approach, he has managed safety and system assurance, requirements management, reliability and maintainability for Aircraft and Helicopter systems, Tunnel systems, Rail Systems, Rolling Stock, Station Systems etc.  

Alessandro has a demonstrated capability to work in a multi-disciplinary environment and with multiple stakeholders at different levels and to lead a team’s effort to achieve project goals in an efficient and timely manner.  

Alex Krensel

Senior Systems Engineer - Requirements & Safety Assurance

Alex is a highly experienced systems engineer with over 30 years' expertise in defence, transport, telecommunications, and construction industries, specializing in requirements management, configuration management, and safety assurance for complex rail infrastructure projects. Currently working across multiple major transport projects including Parramatta Light Rail and Sydney Metro City & Southwest, he brings deep technical expertise in functional safety (IEC 61508 certified) and has held critical roles including Requirements and Configuration Manager for Sydney Metro Northwest and Systems Integration Lead for John Holland on projects worth over $3 billion. His comprehensive experience spans the entire project lifecycle from concept design through commissioning and operational readiness, with particular strengths in test management, verification and validation, and independent safety assessment of SIL-rated systems. Alex's proven track record in managing concurrent complex projects while maintaining rigorous safety and quality standards positions him as an essential technical leader for TfNSW's critical infrastructure delivery programs.

Amanda Murney

Systems Engineering and Assurance

Amanda Murney is a Systems Engineer and Assurance Manager with over 10 years' experience in Systems Engineering, Assurance, Safety and RAM. She has worked in complex developmental projects, rolling stock and rail infrastructure, technical project management, personnel management, resource and asset management, and performing at optimal capability in high-pressure environments.  

Amanda possesses high level of management, communication, interpersonal and relationship building skills and demonstrates astute judgement, problem solving and decision making. She is a highly motivated, self-starter with an exceptional work ethic, consistently driving positive, productive change and delivering timely results in line with organisational intent.

Angus Carter

Program Manager - Defence & Infrastructure Transitions

Angus is an accomplished program manager and defence veteran with over 30 years' experience spanning military logistics, infrastructure project delivery, and asset management across defence, government, and major infrastructure sectors. As a former Major in the Royal Australian Corps of Transport, he brings unique expertise in establishing operational frameworks and mobilising high-performing teams, having successfully led complex transitions including North East Link, Cross River Rail ($5.4B), and Queensland Hydro infrastructure projects. His proven ability to manage multi-million-dollar programs combines a deep understanding of defence requirements with extensive commercial experience in procurement, stakeholder management across Federal and State governments, and facility operations. Angus's distinctive capability to bridge military and civilian infrastructure environments, coupled with his track record in implementing best-practice asset management systems and maintenance programs, positions him as an ideal leader for defence facilities management transitions and complex transport infrastructure mobilisation.

Heidi Vestergaard

Organisational Development & Change Management Specialist

Heidi is a seasoned organisational development professional with extensive experience in talent management, change leadership, and cultural transformation across major Australian corporations, including Slater and Gordon, Orica, Oil Search, and Australia Post. With qualifications including a Master of Organisation Dynamics and Prosci certification, she brings deep expertise in designing and implementing enterprise-wide capability frameworks, leading complex technology transformations including SuccessFactors implementations, and driving engagement strategies that deliver measurable business outcomes. Her proven track record spans end-to-end project management of organisational change initiatives, from stakeholder engagement and process redesign to training delivery and benefits realisation, with particular strength in bridging the gap between strategic objectives and operational execution. Heidi's unique combination of technical project management skills, deep understanding of human dynamics, and experience leading transformational change in complex operational environments makes her an invaluable asset for managing the organisational and people aspects of TfNSW's infrastructure programs.

John Galligan

Infrastructure Transitions & Defence Engineering Specialist

John is a highly experienced infrastructure professional and former Royal Australian Air Force engineer with over 30 years' leadership in defence operations, asset management, and major infrastructure transitions. Currently managing critical transitions for projects including Logan and Gold Coast Faster Rail ($5.75BN), Cross River Rail ($5.4BN), and Kangaroo Point Bridge ($299M), he brings unique expertise in mobilising teams, establishing operational frameworks, and managing complex facility portfolios across defence and civilian sectors. His 18-year RAAF career, including roles as EOD Capability Manager and Aircraft Stores Clearance Flight Commander, provides a deep understanding of defence requirements, security protocols, and maintenance standards essential for critical infrastructure. John's proven track record in collaborative contracting, stakeholder management across all levels of government, and the implementation of best-practice asset management systems positions him as an invaluable leader for TfNSW's complex infrastructure transitions and defence-related facility management requirements.

Laurie Cooper

Projects and Portfolio Manager

Commercially astute, passionate, accountable and highly capable client-side Portfolio and Senior Project Manager with 25+ years’ experience successfully leading the development and execution of complex multi-disciplinary infrastructure portfolios and projects through all lifecycle phases.  In addition to managing the implementation of portfolios and projects, over the past 14 years I have specialised in guiding projects through the development phase, from ideation, optioneering, and feasibility to final investment decision (FID.

Mark Somogyi

Strategic Technology Leader - Tolling Systems & Digital Transformation

Mark is an experienced strategic and operational technology leader with over 20 years of expertise in delivering large-scale platform transformations and enabling enterprise growth in the transport sector. As former GM of Tolling and Customer Platforms at Transurban, he successfully led the GLIDe platform supporting 2.1 million daily road trips and 8 million customers, while driving the integration of six major road assets including WestConnex and managing the migration of over 3 million customers to new core platforms. His proven ability to deliver operational excellence through automation and cloud technologies resulted in platform costs remaining flat while expanding operational capabilities by 25%, achieving significant cost savings while maintaining exceptional system availability and performance. Mark's unique combination of strategic vision, technical depth across infrastructure and application services, and demonstrated success in leading multi-disciplinary teams through complex transformations makes him invaluable for TfNSW's technology-enabled transport infrastructure initiatives.

Nicole Caranguian

Technology Innovation & Digital Transformation Leader

Nicole is a highly motivated executive innovation leader with over 20 years' experience in Defence and National Security, currently serving as Technology Consultant and Director of Applications and Integrations for CBS Group/Defence Housing Australia. With extensive experience spanning Defence ICT innovation, cyber assurance, and strategic growth in defence and national security sectors, she has successfully managed digital transformation portfolios, including the delivery of PROTECTED O365 to support business continuity and the pioneering implementation of emerging technologies such as Microsoft HoloLens 2. Her unique combination of Defence communications and IT expertise, coupled with her extensive global network of government and industry professionals, enables her to drive innovative technology solutions while ensuring security and regulatory compliance. Nicole's proven track record in managing teams of 40+ technical staff, developing strategic roadmaps for artificial intelligence adoption, and delivering complex ICT programs positions her as an asset in digital transformation and secure technology initiatives.

Richard Rao

Systems Integration Manager

An experienced Program Director, Systems Engineer and Business Development professional with analysis, business and delivery management experience in the telecommunications, infrastructure and financial sectors. I thrive on setting up and leading teams and processes to consistently deliver solutions on-time and budget in complex and challenging environments. My direct and focussed style is aimed at removing blockers and holding all stakeholders critical to success accountable for their contributions to deliver the desired business outcomes. I couple this with a strong sense of ownership and accountability, leading by example to create a high performance culture.

Scott Lovell

Strategic Technology Leader - Tolling Systems & Digital Transformation

Scott is a highly skilled Systems, Aerospace and Electrical Engineer with extensive expertise in systems safety design and assurance across rail infrastructure, road tunnels, and air services. With qualifications including a master’s degrees in systems engineering and aerospace engineering, he brings unique value from his defence aviation background to civil infrastructure projects, having successfully transitioned from managing CH-47F helicopter modifications to leading safety assurance for major transport projects. His recent consultancy work with CBS Group has encompassed critical safety roles on Sydney Metro Rail, Victorian Regional Rail Revival Program, and TfNSW tunnel network safety assurance, demonstrating his versatility across diverse transport modes. Scott's deep technical knowledge in avionics, electrical systems, and electromagnetic environmental effects, combined with his proven project management capabilities, positions him as an exceptional asset for complex infrastructure projects requiring rigorous safety protocols and systems integration.

Scott Tapley

Senior Manager - Major Projects & Technology Integration

Scott is an experienced senior manager with over two decades of delivering business and engineering programs across the energy, infrastructure, resources, and automotive industries, and is currently serving as the Major Projects Engagement Manager at Transurban. Responsible for overseeing technology solutions for the $6.5B West Gate Tunnel Project, he has demonstrated exceptional capability in bridging strategic advisory and practical delivery, successfully integrating complex operational control systems and leading enterprise-wide transformation programs. His proven track record includes leading the commercial development for WestConnex ($9.3B acquisition), developing Tolling as a Service proposals, and implementing major ERP transformations at Origin Energy ($400M program), demonstrating deep expertise in stakeholder management, commercial negotiations, and technical translation. Scott's unique ability to solve complex cross-functional challenges, combined with his Roads Australia Fellowship and proven success in delivering technology-enabled infrastructure projects, makes him invaluable to TfNSW's major transport projects that require sophisticated systems integration and commercial acumen.

Simon Phillips

Projects Delivery Specialist

Simon's career is built around successfully delivering projects, programs and portfolios in a diverse range of industries and environments, accountable for projects and programs valued at $100m+ and for portfolios that span multiple continents and include 2500+ projects. This breadth of experience allows Simon to identify and implement solutions that often emerge organically within an organisation. 

A strong engineering background, in combination with 10 years in management consulting, means Simon can seek fact-based data to support problem identification and solutions that generate ongoing business benefits. These skills have been extended in recent roles to include the building of executive dashboards and reporting mechanisms to identify and communicate core problems and recommended solutions. 

Supplementary team mapping to potential activities required by TfNSW

Listed below are sample projects where CBS Group’s Supplementary Team can provide the additional capacity and required expertise to define and deliver TfNSW’s target outcomes in a timely manner.  CBS Group’s Core Team provide oversight, guidance and specialist support to the Supplementary Team to ensure successful delivery.   

Note:  Nick Patras is available from July 2026.
